You will have an opportunity to collaborate with a wide variety of stakeholders, both locally and globally. **Our Benefits Include** * Attractive salary * Performance based bonus scheme * Permanent contract * Rewards scheme * Private Medical Insurance * Competitive maternity benefits of 12 weeks paid Leave * Life assurance **Requirements** * Fluency in English and the required role language * General country specific knowledge for the country you will support * Be eligible to work in Ireland (Stamp 4, Stamp 1G or EU passport) * Computer fluency * Excellent verbal and written communication skills. Customer engagement is the critical part of this role * Review data to assess accuracy against client policies * Engage with business, predominantly via outbound phone calls, to verify their information and update client platforms as appropriate. * Adhere to all compliance requirements * Ability to work effectively in a dynamic and fast paced environment Key success factors for this role are adaptability and flexibility, attention to detail and an ability to focus on repetitive tasks, excellent communication skills, an ability to make judgement and a problem\-solving nature. We offer interesting work at a globally admired technology company, an attractive compensation package, valuable training and career progression opportunities across the globe. Previous experience in a similar role is desired but not required.

title: Data Product Owner
description: We’re looking for a Data Product Owner to lead the strategy, delivery and evolution of customer and consumer\-focused data products within a growing data and analytics function. This role combines product ownership, stakeholder management and data delivery leadership, working across business and technology teams to build scalable, insight\-driven solutions that support strategic decision\-making. **The role** * Define and own the vision, strategy and roadmap for customer and consumer data products * Work closely with business stakeholders to identify opportunities where data can drive value and operational improvement * Translate business requirements into scalable data, BI, analytics and AI solutions * Prioritise delivery roadmaps and manage product backlogs aligned to business priorities * Lead cross\-functional teams delivering secure, reliable and high\-value data products * Collaborate with platform, governance, architecture and security teams * Drive adoption of self\-service analytics, automation and AI\-driven insights * Act as the escalation point for product quality, performance and delivery issues * Identify opportunities to improve existing capabilities through emerging technologies **Experience** * 10\+ years’ experience across data product management, business intelligence, data engineering or analytics delivery * Proven experience managing end\-to\-end data solution delivery * Strong understanding of BI, analytics and data engineering platforms * Hands\-on exposure to a variety of data tools and platforms * Experience working with customer or consumer data environments * Strong stakeholder management skills with the ability to influence senior business leaders * Comfortable translating complex technical concepts into clear business outcomes * Strong analytical and problem\-solving capabilities with a product\-led mindset * Experience working in Agile delivery environments is highly advantageous

title: Data and Insights Engineer 97528
description: Before you apply This job opportunity is open to both HSE and non\-HSE applicants. Reference 97528 Category Management/Admin/ICT Grade Grade VII 0582 Advertisement source Section 38 Advertisement Type External Important Information The HSE is advertising this job on behalf of a publicly funded organisation. This job is not with the HSE. The terms and conditions of this job may be different from the HSE’s terms and conditions. Health region HSE Dublin and North East County * Dublin North Location Clontarf Recruiter Central Remedial Clinic Contract type * Fixed Term Wholetime Post specific related information Data \& Insights Engineer CRC Clontarf Fixed Term Contract (2 years), Full\-Time (35 hrs per week) We are delighted to offer the opportunity for a Data \& Insights Engineer to join the CRC team and help drive the organisation’s journey through change. Our 2025\-2030 strategy sets out the renewed purpose, bold ambitions and values\-led direction of the organisation. The strategic direction of the organisation is to provide our service users with the right supports at the right time. Together with individuals, families and our partners, we are aiming to shape a world where everyone is valued and supported to live the life they choose. This is an excellent opportunity for a skilled and purpose\-led data practitioner to take the lead in developing the CRC’s data engineering capabilities and put data\-driven insights and decision\-making tools at the heart of the organisation’s efforts to shape a data\-informed culture that delivers better outcomes for those we serve. This is a foundational role focused on building practical, sustainable data and reporting capability in an organisation at an early stage of data maturity. The emphasis is on pragmatism, usability and impact rather than large‑scale enterprise data platforms. **Responsibilities** Beyond technical expertise, we are looking for someone who is aligned with our values and the successful candidate’s responsibilities will include: Person\-Centredness: Develop data structures that provide insights for service improvement and build co\-design into how the CRC manages and uses data. Quality: Lead on development of data structures and dashboards that support quality improvement initiatives and align with best\-practice for data governance and compliance Respect: Communicate effectively with a range of technical and non\-technical stakeholders, presenting complex information in a clear and understandable way Courage: Challenge assumptions and support organisational and individual change based on data and evidence\-based insights Collaboration: Work with and upskill colleagues and other stakeholders to ensure sustainable management and use of data across the organisation Stewardship: Ensure the responsible use of data to inform organisational decision making around resources, assets and service delivery Message from the Head of Strategy \& Innovation: At the CRC, we are entering an exciting phase of transformation, one where data has the power to do more than guide decisions. It can genuinely change lives. This role is central to building a culture where insight drives action and where evidence strengthens the services we provide. As our Data \& Insights Engineer, you will help shape how we understand our work, how we plan for the future, and how we deliver meaningful, person‑centred improvements. We are looking for someone who sees the potential in data, someone who can turn information into clarity, and clarity into impact. If you are motivated by purpose and excited by the chance to build capability from the ground up, we would be delighted to hear from you. **Essential** Qualifications, Skills and Experience * A degree (a minimum of NFQ Level 8 or greater) or equivalent professional qualification in a relevant technical discipline such as Computer Science, Engineering, or Data Analytics. * Minimum of 3\-5 years of experience in data engineering, data analytics or a related field. * Hands‑on experience designing and maintaining practical data pipelines and transformations, typically using SQL, Power BI dataflows, and existing enterprise systems. * Hands\-on experience with scripting languages e.g. VBA, Python * Experience designing and maintaining data models that support consistent KPIs and self‑service reporting. * Proficiency in establishing dataflows, data pipelines, API integration and modern ETL/ELT practices. * Strong SQL skills and experience with data lakes, data warehouses and relational databases. * Understanding of data governance principles and compliance requirements (GDPR knowledge essential). * Demonstrated understanding of using KPI metrics to drive an organisation’s performance. * Excellent problem\-solving and troubleshooting skills. * Ability to collaborate effectively and upskill technical and non\-technical stakeholders as appropriate. * Strong written and verbal communication skills and ability to present complex business information to a range of non\-technical stakeholders. * Strong project management and organizational skills. Desirable * Experience of working within a comparable environment, e.g. not for profit/public sector organisation, healthcare, disability services. * Experience working with legacy or fragmented data landscapes. * Experience with AI features in Power BI. * Understanding of financial accounting principles. Detailed List of Responsibilities \& Deliverables The responsibilities outlined below are indicative of the scope of the role currently envisioned but may be adapted as required in line with the CRC’s organisational strategy and increased visibility of relevant information. Therefore, a degree of flexibility will be required from the successful candidate. * Data Foundations \& Engineering * Design, implement and maintain robust data pipelines, dataflows and storage structures to ensure accurate and timely reporting * Ensure integration of data from various sources, including on premises databases, CRM applications and SharePoint Excel files * Design and implement appropriate data structures and models to improve performance, consistency and reuse across datasets and dashboards * Support the organisation’s drive for process improvement and efficiency, including automation of data feeds, quality checks and proactive troubleshooting of issues * Reporting, Dashboards \& Data‑Driven Insights * Act as a specialist resource to deliver data\-based, intelligent reports and provide appropriate advice to clinical teams and support functions on managing and interpreting these and applying data\-informed insights to manage organisational / team / individual performance * Lead the development and ongoing improvement of operational and strategic dashboards that support performance monitoring, service design, and decision‑making. * Support operational and strategic decision\-making by developing data flows that drive continuous improvement and analysis of information underpinning service design and delivery * Use data to identify trends, opportunities and risks for services, including supporting cost improvement programmes and idea generation * Proactively communicate findings to relevant parties where developments and changes are needed. * Data Governance, Quality \& Compliance * Ensure data structures, pipelines and reporting align with the organisation’s data governance, quality and compliance standards. * Promote consistent use of definitions, KPIs, and metrics across reports and dashboards. * Ensure attention to detail and adherence to procedures and standards within the data function. * Support responsible data stewardship to inform decision‑making around resources, assets and service delivery, e.g. in service or workforce planning or business cases * Enablement, Training \& Organisational Adoption * Deliver training, guidance and communications to encourage technical and non\-technical stakeholders to engage with the dashboards and reports * Work with colleagues across the organisation to build data literacy and support sustainable, self‑service use of data where appropriate. * Document data solutions and processes to support sustainable knowledge transfer and continuity, and reduce reliance on technical experts * Collaborate with stakeholders to embed data‑informed approaches into everyday planning and performance discussions. * Service Improvement, Co‑Design \& Change * Participate in the co\-design of service improvements through provision and interpretation of qualitative and quantitative data. * Practice and promote continuous improvement to enable quality services for internal and external stakeholders and an awareness and appreciation of the service user. * Embrace and promote the organisation’s transformation and change agenda, supporting others through change. * Assist with data and reporting input into the identification of new systems, working in partnership with IT and external vendors to identify solutions that align with the CRC’s requirements for IT upgrades. * Demonstrate flexibility and initiative during challenging times and an ability to persevere despite setbacks. Informal enquiries to Rory O'Sullivan, Head of Strategy \& Innovation, Tel 087 709 6191 or by email: rosullivan@crc.ie Department of Health Salary Scale, Grade VII \- code 0582 (Salary Scale \- €60,613 to €78,795 – Inc. 2 LSI’s). Applicants must demonstrate in their CV and supporting documentation how they meet the above criteria as short\-listing will apply. Applicants must be eligible to work in Ireland at time of application. Applications must be received on or before Thursdsay, 21st May 2026\. Please submit your application via the 'Apply Now' button below. PLEASE NOTE \- Applicants must be eligible to work in Ireland at date of application. Data Protection: Please refer to crc\_job\_application\_privacy\_notice.pdf to learn more about how we handle your personal data and the rights that you have during the recruitment cycle. Closing date 21/05/2026 00:00:00 Proposed interview date TBC Informal enquiries Rory O'Sullivan, Head of Strategy \& Innovation, Tel 087 709 6191 or by email: rosullivan@crc.ie External link https://www.rezoomo.com/job/97528/

Robert Walters Operations Limited is an employment business and employment agency and welcomes applications from all candidates

title: Deskside Engineer
description: Fully onsite based Dublin 18 and Dublin 2 Auxilion a leading provider of IT outsourcing and IT managed service solutions. As part of the Auxilion organization you will work directly on a client site responding to internal customer service requests needing technical support for hardware/software issues. You will be working closely with a wider team across Ireland to provide the best possible technical service and customer experience that the client requires. The IT Deskside Engineer is responsible for providing on\-site technical support to end\-users, addressing immediate technical concerns, and ensuring that all IT equipment is functioning optimally. 2 to 3 years Deskside experience. The Deskside Engineer will be responsible for providing high\-quality technical support to end\-users, troubleshooting hardware and software issues, and ensuring the smooth operation of IT services within the organization. The ideal candidate will possess strong problem\-solving abilities, excellent communication skills, and a customer\-focused attitude. **Key Responsibilities** * Provide deskside support for hardware, software, and networking issues. * Set up, configure, and maintain desktops, laptops, printers, and peripherals. * Resolve incidents and service requests within agreed SLAs. * Deliver face‑to‑face support through a walk‑up Techzone. * Perform initial investigation, diagnosis, and resolution of incidents. * Support problem management through trend analysis and issue identification. * Install and verify software, updates, and patches. * Maintain accurate asset tracking for all managed equipment. * Support workplace recovery activities. * Maintain knowledge base articles and documentation. * Assist with internal and external audits as required. Excellent knowledge of desktop, laptop and mobile device hardware and Microsoft OS products including Windows 10/11, and Microsoft 365 Networking technologies (WAN/LAN) \& understanding of networking protocols. **Key Skills include** * Windows 10/11 \& Microsoft Office * Microsoft 365 (O365\) * Microsoft Teams * Microsoft Active Directory * Intune / Endpoint Management * ServiceNow (ITSM) * Hardware, software, and networking support * Experience supporting enterprise Windows environments **IT Service Management Skills:** * ITIL‑aligned Incident, Request, and Problem Management * Strong troubleshooting and root‑cause analysis abilities * Knowledge base maintenance and documentation * SLA/KPI awareness and service‑focused approach * Strong troubleshooting and problem‑solving skills. * Excellent communication and customer‑service mindset. * Experience supporting Windows environments, hardware, and networking. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team. * A proactive, organised, and detail‑focused approach. **Our Company, Auxilion – About Us** Work matters. It’s where we spend a third of our lives. At Auxilion we strive to be a great place to work, with career opportunities and development of people in our DNA. Our competitive advantage is how we support our clients on their journey. Our people have a passion for their work, our culture and values. Whether your focus is technical, sales, finance or anything in\-between, our roles aim to provide each person with meaningful impact and plenty of space to grow.

title: DevOps Engineer
description: **At Semperis, our mission is to be a Force for Good. Starting with being a great place to work.** We believe that when people feel valued, supported, and empowered, they do their best work. That’s why we focus on creating an employee experience rooted in purpose, growth, and balance. Semperis has been recognized as one of America’s Fastest\-Growing Cybersecurity Companies by the Inc. 5000, a DUNS 100 Top Startup to Work For, and a multi\-year Inc. Best Workplace awardee. **What We Are Looking For** We are looking for a Junior DevOps Engineer to join our Engineering team and grow into a key contributor on our product critical path: deployment, orchestration, observability, and secure communication. Working alongside senior engineers, you will learn and apply modern DevOps practices while contributing to real\-world production systems from day one. **What You Will Be Doing** * Write programs/scripts and automation to streamline routine operational tasks * Assist in building, maintaining, and improving CI/CD pipelines and release processes across product teams * Support cloud infrastructure management using Infrastructure\-as\-Code (Terraform/Terragrunt, Helm charts, Bicep/ARM) under senior guidance * Help operate and maintain Kubernetes clusters (AKS) and supporting services * Contribute to GitOps workflows using Argo CD for declarative, auditable deployments * Help maintain observability tooling — metrics, logging, alerting, tracing (Prometheus, Grafana, Azure Monitor, OpenTelemetry) * Manage on\-prem and cloud resources, assist with cost monitoring, and support infrastructure health checks across Azure environments * Participate in incident management — join on\-call rotation with senior engineer mentorship, assist in triage, and contribute to post\-incident reviews * Collaborate with product engineering, QA, security, and support teams as part of microservices, event\-driven architectures, or distributed systems * A degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience) * Security\-first and developer experience mindset — you care about doing things the right way, not just the fast way **Non\-Negotiables** * Must be in Ireland with valid work visa or citizenship * Must have solid hands\-on experience with programming and foundational experience with cloud/infrastructure concepts — through work or valid internships * Must demonstrate clear communication skills with team members, title: Digital Marketing - Acquisition - Dutch Speaker
description: Due to continued expansion and growth, Accenture is currently recruiting for a Partner Manager to drive the rollout of a unique digital marketing advertising solution to EU markets. We are partnering with Pinterest to ensure businesses are set up for success on the platform. You will support new clients from the very start of their Pinterest journey and help to educate, inspire and help advertisers define the best type of campaigns to drive results and achieve their goals. This is an opportunity to work with one of the fastest\-growing players in social media, with the support of a leading global professional services company. Our high\-performing Pinterest team has a proud track record. We maintain a positive, high\-performing team environment by looking out for each other, cherishing diversity and taking time to stop and celebrate success together regularly. **Responsibilities** - We put the power in your hands; to identify, research and contact potential new partner businesses that you believe Pinterest’s unique business proposition could bring value to, delivering personalised product pitches. - Setting the advertiser up for success before passing over to a colleague in Account management to be scaled - You will also work with businesses and brands that have already started advertising on the platform to understand their strategic and tactical goals and co\-create the right marketing plan to achieve them. - Gain an in\-depth understanding of partners' business goals in their respective markets, offering educated insights to help them to succeed. - Developing and growing long term relationships as the ‘face’ \& first point of contact for your clients. - Work toward fixed KPIs \& a quarterly sales target. - Work collaboratively with the wider sales team in order to share (and gain) valuable knowledge on your role. **Basic Qualifications** - Completed High School Diploma (Irish Leaving cert or equivalent) **Qualifications** - At least one year of experience in a target\-driven, customer\-facing role (sales is preferred). - In\-depth, demonstrated knowledge of digital marketing, including understanding of metrics such as CPA, CPC, CPM and ROAS. - Demonstrated experience using analytical approaches to solving business problems. - Demonstrable ability to train and educate advertisers on solutions including how to create and optimise campaigns. - Demonstrable ability to understand, validate, and overcome customer objections by crafting a compelling marketing plan to use Pinterest’s platform \& functionality to achieve advertisers goals. - Ability to learn quickly and maintain current, comprehensive knowledge of the client’s products and services. - Tech\-savvy with an affinity for innovative and emerging technology. - Self\-reliant, adaptable, decisive, and professional; able to effectively multi\-task in a dynamic environment. - Proven experience in working toward quarterly goals, in a KPI\-driven environment. **What do we offer:** - Hybrid role (3 days working from Office and 2 days working from home) - Monday to Friday - Health Insurance - Social team events (indoor and outdoor) - 20 days annual leave \+ 2 Company days \+ 2 volunteers. - Recognition points which you can spend on shopping or vouchers. *Please also note that Accenture is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all sections of society and does not discriminate on grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability, age, citizenship, marital, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation, or gender identity.* *Please advise if you have any particular requirements, so we can look into arranging reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process. You can contact us at* *accessIErecruitment@accenture.com*

title: Doctor of Lifestyle and Functional Medicine
description: ### **About the Role** We are seeking a motivated and forward\-thinking Doctor of Lifestyle, Functional and Precision Medicine team. This is an exceptional opportunity for a doctor with a strong interest in integrative health, chronic disease prevention, anti\-ageing science and root\-cause medicine. You will work in a modern, doctor\-led private clinic focused on health optimisation, evidence\-based personalised care and long\-term patient wellbeing. This role is ideal for a clinician who is passionate about expanding their expertise in Lifestyle, Functional Medicine, Genomics, Nutrition, Metabolism, Endocrinology, internal Medicine, Male and Female Health and holistic approaches that complement mainstream medical practice. ### **Key Responsibilities** * Deliver high\-quality patient care across lifestyle, functional and precision medicine programmes. * Conduct detailed consultations including metabolic, hormonal, longevity and performance\-focused assessments. * Interpret advanced biomarkers including endocrinology, biochemistry, haematology, oxidative stress, mitochondrial health and metabolic markers. * Develop personalised treatment plans incorporating nutrition, exercise, supplementation, hormone balance, and evidence\-based therapeutics. * Support male and female hormone health, cardiometabolic optimisation, gut health and chronic disease risk reduction. * Utilise genomic and DNA testing as part of personalised risk stratification and long\-term clinical management. (professional training offered) * Work collaboratively with senior physicians, nutritionists, IV therapy nurses and allied professionals. Maintain accurate clinical documentation and follow clinical governance, safety and ethical guidelines. Participate in continuous learning, research initiatives and case reviews. ### **What We Offer** **A competitive remuneration package** depending on experience and qualifications, plus a range of benefits designed to support your development and long\-term career in Functional and Precision Medicine: **Professional Development \& Training** * Full training in genetic and genomic medicine for use in clinical practice. * Advanced learning in telomere biology, NAD pathways, oxidative stress, metabolic health and anti\-ageing science. * Paid access to professional CPD, conferences and evidence\-based Functional Medicine modules. * Mentorship from highly experienced clinicians in precision and integrative medicine. **Attractive Work Environment** * Doctor\-led, evidence\-based clinic with strong patient outcomes and high satisfaction rates. * Protected consultation times for complex patients. * Strong administrative and professional staff support. * Modern diagnostic protocols and access to advanced biomarker testing. * Opportunity to influence new protocols, research and clinical service development. **Career Progression** * Clear pathway to Senior Functional / Lifestyle Medicine Physician. * Opportunities to develop specialist clinics (hormone health, metabolic medicine, longevity, DNA\-based personalised medicine). * Potential for performance\-based bonuses or leadership roles as the service expands. Apply today if you are interested in this position. * Training or certification in Functional Medicine, Lifestyle Medicine or Integrative Medicine (e.g. IFM, BSLM, IBLM). * Experience in endocrinology, metabolic medicine, sports medicine, nutrition or gut health. * Exposure to genomic or DNA\-based clinical interpretation. * Experience in private practice or complex chronic disease management. * At least 1–5 years clinical experience post\-qualification (Junior Doctor or Core GP level). * Passion for anti\-ageing, longevity science and proactive healthcare. * Excellent communication skills, professionalism and patient\-centred approach. * Commitment to continuous learning and evidence\-based practice.

title: EV Operations Engineer

This role blends hardware, software, and customer support, giving you hands\-on exposure to connected devices and real\-world infrastructure. **Responsibilities:** * Provide **1st and 2nd line operations support** across EV charging hardware and software platforms * Troubleshoot issues related to **hardware, connectivity, networking, and user access** * Manage incoming support tickets via ticketing systems * Monitor live systems and proactively respond to alerts or faults * Support **remote diagnostics** , onboarding, and customer issue resolution * Coordinate with internal engineering teams and third\-party partners to escalate complex issues * Manage and support EV chargers installed at customer homes and commercial sites * Occasionally travel to customer sites to diagnose and resolve technical issues * Maintain accurate documentation and contribute to the internal knowledge base * Take full ownership and accountability for customer issues from start to resolution **Required:** * **Experience** in technical support, IT support, or field support roles * Degree in **Electrical Engineering, IT or a related field** * Strong troubleshooting skills across **hardware and software environments** * Experience using ticketing systems. * Excellent communication skills with a strong **customer\-first mindset** * Professional, outgoing personality with strong interpersonal skills * Knowledge in EV, IoT, telecoms, or energy sector is a plus * **Full, clean driver’s licence**

title: Field Services Engineer
description: JO\-2605\-565230 Field Service Engineer – Semiconductor Industry Location: Leixlip, Co. Kildare Job Type: Full\-Time Permanent Cpl are once again delighted to partner exclusively with a long\-established service provider supporting the semiconductor manufacturing industry in North Kildare. Due to continued expansion on one of Europe’s largest semiconductor manufacturing facilities, we are currently hiring multiple Field Service Engineers for full\-time permanent positions based on\-site in Leixlip, Co. Kildare. This is an excellent opportunity to join a highly technical and fast\-paced engineering environment, working alongside experienced electronic, automation, mechanical and electrical engineering professionals supporting advanced semiconductor manufacturing equipment and systems. The successful candidates will play a key role in the installation, qualification, maintenance, troubleshooting and optimisation of highly sophisticated semiconductor capital equipment within a world\-class cleanroom manufacturing facility. These positions may also involve international travel to the USA or Taiwan for OEM training and certification programs. A full driving licence (Irish, UK, EU or International) is required, as company transport may be provided during overseas training assignments. \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ Key Responsibilities • Install, commission and qualify semiconductor manufacturing equipment in accordance with OEM specifications and cleanroom procedures. • Perform preventative maintenance, calibration and equipment servicing activities to maximise uptime and reliability. • Troubleshoot and diagnose complex electrical, mechanical, automation and process\-related faults on semiconductor capital equipment. • Carry out corrective maintenance and support root cause analysis (RCA) investigations to implement sustainable technical solutions. • Support equipment upgrades, retrofits and continuous improvement projects across the manufacturing facility. • Work closely with process engineers, equipment engineers and production teams to optimise equipment performance and yield. • Maintain detailed service documentation, maintenance records and technical reports in line with quality and compliance standards. • Ensure strict adherence to EHS, cleanroom and contamination control procedures at all times. • Provide technical support and operational guidance to customers and internal stakeholders where required. • Participate in on\-call support and escalation activities to support critical manufacturing operations. • Liaise with global engineering and technical support teams to resolve complex technical issues. • Support spare parts management and ensure availability of critical components for service activities. • Identify opportunities to improve equipment reliability, efficiency and customer satisfaction. • Support compliance with ISO, GMP and semiconductor manufacturing standards and procedures. \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ Skills \& Requirements • Minimum Level 7 qualification in Electrical Engineering, Electronic Engineering, Automation, Robotics, Mechatronics, Mechanical Engineering or a related engineering discipline. • Alternatively, a Level 6 trade qualification with significant relevant semiconductor, manufacturing or equipment maintenance experience will also be considered. • Previous experience in semiconductor manufacturing, electronics, automation, medical device, pharmaceutical or high\-tech manufacturing environments would be advantageous. • Recent engineering graduates with strong technical aptitude and a genuine interest in semiconductor technology are also encouraged to apply. • Strong troubleshooting and fault\-finding skills across electrical, electronic and mechanical systems. • Experience working with precision equipment, diagnostic tools, test fixtures and technical documentation. • Good understanding of cleanroom protocols and high\-volume manufacturing environments would be beneficial. • Strong communication, organisational and problem\-solving skills. • Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a high\-performing engineering team. • Proficiency in Microsoft Office and Windows\-based software systems. \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ What’s on Offer • Full\-time permanent employment contracts • Opportunity to work on one of Europe’s most advanced semiconductor manufacturing sites • Structured OEM training and international certification programs • Career progression within a environment \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ If you are interested in this excellent opportunity and would like to be considered for these full\-time title: Finance & Reporting Analyst
description: We’ve an exciting opportunity for a Finance \& Reporting Analyst to join our IT Finance team. This role plays an important part in delivering accurate reporting, strong financial control and data‑led insight to support decision making. In this role, you’ll manage the end‑to‑end reporting cycle, from month‑end close through to forecasting and analysis, working closely with IT and Finance partners. It’s a great opportunity to build your experience in a data‑rich environment, developing both your technical skills and commercial awareness. You’ll play a key role in understanding cost drivers and supporting better planning and forecasting. It’s a great opportunity to build your commercial impact in a visible and evolving area of the business. **Duties \& Responsibilities** * Manage the IT month‑end close process, ensuring accurate and timely financial reporting * Analyse financial results and variances, helping to explain key drivers and trends in a clear and simple way * Support budgeting, forecasting and business case development across IT cost areas * Work with stakeholders across IT, Procurement and Finance to ensure accurate data and consistent reporting * Identify opportunities to improve processes, enhance reporting, and increase efficiency through automation **Skills \& Experience** * Experience in a finance role such as accounts payable, accounts technician, or financial analysis, with a willingness to develop further * Strong analytical skills with the ability to work with large and complex data sets * Understanding of financial reporting, month‑end processes, budgeting or forecasting * Strong Excel skills and ideally experience with tools such as Power BI or similar * Strong communication skills with the ability to explain financial insights to non‑finance audiences. **What will you get for this role?** Our purpose \- with you today, for a better tomorrow – is a promise we make to our colleagues too. And one of the ways we live up to that promise is by investing in you. We have so much to offer when it comes to being an Aviva colleague. * A competitive market leading salary depending on skills, experience, and qualifications. * Generous pension (employee contribution matching of up to 14% if you contribute 8%). * Annual performance related bonus and pay review. * Holiday allowance of 25 days plus bank holidays, and the option to buy/sell up to 5 additional days. * Aviva Matching Share Plan and Save As You Earn scheme and Approved Profit Share Scheme. * TaxSaver Travel Scheme. * Up to 40% discount for some Aviva products plus discounts for Friends and Family. * Professional qualification support and transparent career progression plans. * Brilliantly supportive policies including parental and carer’s leave. * Flexible benefits to suit you, including sustainability options such as cycle to work . * Make a difference, be part of our Aviva Communities and use your paid volunteering days to help others. * We take your wellbeing seriously with lots of support and tools. **Aviva is for Everyone** Our diversity and inclusion policies and initiatives are shaping an environment where everyone feels welcome regardless of age, disability, race, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, religion, culture, sexual orientation, national origin, marital status, pregnancy, maternity or those with other caring responsibilities. Our approach helps to ensure that Aviva is a place which values difference and provides equal opportunities for all. We flex locations, hours and working patterns to suit our customers, business, and you. Most of our people are smart working – spending at least 50% of their time in our offices every week \- combining the benefits of flexibility, with time together with colleagues. We prefer all applications to be submitted online, however if you require an alternative method of applying, please contact kate.ohara@aviva.com

title: Financial Accountant
description: * Dublin, Ireland * Leeds, United Kingdom * London, United Kingdom * Full time 40 hours * Fixed Term (Fixed Term) * Flutter Group **Financial Accountant** **Location \-** Dublin/Leeds/London **Hybrid \-** 2 days per week (Dublin only) **Contract Type \-** 2 year FTC Flutter consists of two commercial divisions (FanDuel and International) and our central Flutter Functions; COO, Finance \& Legal. Here in Flutter Functions, we work with colleagues across all our divisions and regions to deliver something we call the Flutter Edge. It’s our competitive advantage, our ‘secret sauce’ which plays a key part in our ongoing success and powers our brands and divisions, through Product, Tech, Expertise and Scale. In Flutter Finance we pride ourselves in providing global expertise to ensure Flutter is financially healthy. Utilizing our Flutter Edge to turbo\-charge our capabilities. We are looking for a Financial Accountant in our Record to Report (RTR) space, that will deliver financial and management information to all internal and external stakeholders. This role will focus predominantly on accounting and reporting for OpEx costs within the GFC team, across Flutter Functions \& UKI. This will include being involved in the management of specific operating expenses and the reporting of monthly results to the business, along with analysis. **What You’ll Do** * Assist with the production of month end numbers for Flutter Functions \& UKI, within Group Reporting deadlines * Operate a robust cost accruals process to ensure that costs are correctly accrued within the time constraints of a fast\-close environment * Review all significant variances for areas of responsibility flagging key variances in advance of close * Standardise, and automate where possible, month end tasks to improve month end timelines * Continuously review processes to find efficiencies in ways of working and opportunities to implement AI initiatives * Complete balance sheet reconciliations within set deadlines * Proactively engage with assigned budget owners to improve processes and procedures around monthly accruals * Continuously review processes and procedures in line with changes in systems and the requirements of the business * Ensure processes and procedures are documented and robust * Proactively engage in cross training initiatives * Other tasks that may be assigned as appropriate * Work collaboratively with related finance teams to achieve wider finance/organisational goals and efficiencies * Pursue opportunities to reduce the monthly close process duration without impacting the quality of the information produced * Ensure processes and procedures within area of responsibility are documented and robust * Work with all teams within the GFC as part of accounts production / close process to ensure accurate and timely accounting information is produced in accordance with their requirements * Support Commercial Finance in the budget and forecasting process to meet Group timelines * Proactively engage with internal and external audits when required * Deliver timely and accurate financial information to the Group reporting team **How You’ll Do It** To succeed in this role, you should have: * A qualification as an accountant with 2\+ years working in a similar environment, demonstrating a proven track record in financial reporting and analysis * Keen attention to detail \& drive to improve quality * Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to challenge stakeholders and take ownership of your personal development * The ability to explain financial concepts to non\-financial staff confidently and professionally * Comfort working with large volumes of data and in highly automated environments * A team\-oriented approach with the ability to thrive in a dynamic environment * Experience with Oracle ERP or other similar financial systems an advantage * Experience in a US GAAP/SOx environment (advantageous) * Excellent proficiency in MS Office, particularly Excel **What’s in it for you** We are a flexible employer; whether you have personal commitments or a hobby that brings you joy, we want you to bring your best self to work and feel empowered to do so. We also like to share our success; after all you make it happen. We have an excellent benefits package that can be personalised to you: * Bonus scheme * Uncapped holiday allowance * Enhanced pension scheme * Private healthcare * Life assurance * Income protection * Hybrid working * £/€1,000 annual self\-development learning fund * Invest via the Flutters Sharesave Scheme * Paid volunteering days * Enhanced parental leave * Wellbeing fund (£/€250 a year) * Recognition programs * Electric car scheme, gym membership, discounts, vouchers and much more! **About Flutter** We are a world leader in online sports betting and iGaming, with a market leading position in the US and across the world. We have an unparalleled portfolio of the most innovative, diverse and distinctive brands including FanDuel, Sky Betting \& Gaming, Sportsbet, PokerStars, Paddy Power, Sisal, tombola, Betfair, MaxBet, Junglee Games and Adjarabet. With our global scale and challenger attitude, through which we excite and entertain our customers, in a safe and sustainable way. Using our collective power, the Flutter Edge, we aim to disrupt the sector, learning from the past to create a better future for our customers, colleagues and communities. We’re working to be an inclusive employer, and we encourage people from all backgrounds, ways of thinking and working to apply. Everyone brings different perspectives and experiences; you don't have to meet all the requirements listed to apply for this role. If you need any adjustments to make this role work for you let us know, and we’ll see how we can accommodate them.

title: Financial and Regulatory Reporting Analyst
description: **Job Title** : Financial and Regulatory Reporting Analyst **Business Unit** : BNP Paribas Security (BP2S) Operations **Contract Type** : Permanent **Location** : Sandyford, Dublin/Parkmore, Galway (Hybrid) **Why work with us?** We are the bank for a changing world and we aim to build positive change together: * We are a team of colleagues who are enthusiastic about what we do and the impact our work has on our clients. We work in a highly collaborative, open\-minded environment that embraces innovation and creativity + We offer competitive remuneration and a comprehensive benefits package, including a discretionary bonus scheme, company pension contribution, comprehensive private healthcare packages, health and fitness subsidy, work\-from\-home subsidy, above statutory days of annual leave and numerous other benefits. + We are strongly committed to wellbeing and work\-life balance, including a 50% hybrid work model (where the role allows), paid family leave, fertility and menopause support — available to support all life stage needs. + Equality, diversity and inclusion are at the core of our culture, and we actively promote equal opportunities through creating or being part of initiatives and programmes: + Aspire Female Leadership Programme, provides development and exposure to high\-potential women in the bank, through targeted workshops, executive coaching and formal mentoring + Proud members of 100 Women in Finance and among the first signatories of Ireland’s Women in Finance charter + Joined the \#JamaisSansElles (Never Without Her) Charter, committing its executive committee to only join public or media events with panels of three or more speakers, if at least one of those speakers is a woman + Development and mobility are actively encouraged. BNP Paribas’ culture is built on purposeful talent development and recognition programmes that foster engagement, inclusive leadership and continuous growth. A few of our programmes include: + Leaders for Tomorrow, a programme that fast\-tracks high\-potential talent through training, development and cross\-functional projects, preparing them for future leadership positions + Local and Global Mentoring initiatives, that pair employees with senior leaders, providing staff with platforms to gain advice on career growth topics + Ambassador Awards employee recognition scheme provides rewards to those who have embodied the Bank’s core values + Local Experts (Champions) Programme, identifying internal subject\-matter experts from specific business areas, who excel at their craft + We are a global company operating in over 60 countries with over 180,000 employees from over 150 nationalities. In Ireland we have approximately 550 employees across Dublin and Galway, working in Corporate Institutional Banking (CIB) and BNP Paribas Security Services (BP2S) and Greenval **Equality and Diversity** BNP Paribas is an equal opportunities employer and proudly cultivates a diverse workforce. We believe that diversity of experience enhances our service offering to clients and leads to a better working environment. We welcome applications from the best candidates regardless of age, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, nationality, disability, sexual orientation, socio\-economic background, parental and caring status, or religious belief. Our Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) play a key role in promoting diversity and inclusion through initiatives, events and advocacy programmes that foster a sense of belonging for all colleagues. In BNP Paribas Ireland, we have dedicated networks for parents and caregivers, LGBTQA\+, staff from diverse cultural and religious backgrounds, alongside forums to discuss CSR and gender related topics. **Sustainable Finance** BNP continues to demonstrate the Bank’s steadfast commitment to supporting its clients in their growth ambitions by expanding market reach across assets, delivering excellence in bond markets, and showing the way in sustainable financing. **Role** * This role sits in the BNP Paribas Securities Services division (BP2S) * As a key member of the team, the objective of this role is to assist in the production of the financial and regulatory reporting obligations of the funds we administer * The core responsibilities require some subject matter expertise with attention to detail, quality control and excellent service delivery * The team works closely with the Operations functions to ensure successful delivery of financial and regulatory reporting deliverables * It is an exciting opportunity for someone with experience in fund administration and an interest in financial/regulatory reporting to be involved with a growing service within our Fund Administration business **K** **ey Responsibilities** * To assist in production of financial and regulatory reporting team obligations (Financial Reporting and Regulatory Reporting) * Ensure all financial reporting procedures are adhered to and ensure compliance with internal and external regulations * To assist in efficiently planning the financial statement preparation and audit process in conjunction with all relevant parties, ensuring that agreed timelines are achieved * Assist with the workflows for all audit engagements, act as both an internal reference and escalation point for audit issues * To keep up to date with all relevant regulatory and accounting changes and assist with re\-engineering of processes where necessary * To liaise with clients and auditors to ensure that the financial statements are audited and filed with the relevant regulatory bodies and Stock Exchanges on a timely basis * Assist with ensuring effective oversight of outsourcing vendors in accordance with Outsourcing Policy and to ensure high service levels are maintained * Preparation and review of client CBI regulatory returns (DIFR, MMIF, Monthly NAV return etc.) * Other ad hoc projects **Qualifications \& Experience Required For This Role** * Finance/Business graduate, Qualified/Part qualified Accountant (ACA, ACCA, CPA or CIMA) an advantage * Minimum of 3 years’ experience working in a financial reporting department, audit experience or fund administration roles * Understanding of accounting standards, primarily IFRS, US GAAP and FRS 102 a benefit * Be able to take responsibility and resolve issues as they arise * Ability to work to tight deadlines * Excellent organisational and Microsoft office skills * Knowledge of Fund Accounting/Investor Services or other accounting platforms an advantage * Excellent communication skills **About BNP Paribas** At BNP Paribas, we center our values on what drives the company forward. Our driving forces are: * Agility: we want to behave more simply to embrace useful innovation and digital transformation * Client Satisfaction: we believe that our success lies in being the customers’ and clients’ preferred choice. We seek to listen carefully to them and work closely with them * Compliance Culture: We believe in promoting clear rules to foster a strong culture of compliance and ethics * Openness: We promote open\-minded attitudes towards our stakeholders. We aim for everyone in Group to feel included, to have their say and be empowered **Our Strengths Are Based On Four Key Areas** * Stability: we build upon our solid, long\-term oriented management, our diversified and integrated business model and our international footprint * Expertise: We build upon our recognized and expanding knowledge of our teams * Responsibility: We build upon our culture of responsibility and integrity to ever better serve the interest of our customers * Good Place to Work: We foster a stimulating workplace where people are treated fairly and with respect **Management excellence** BNP continues to foster top\-class people management practices within our strong people focused culture. BNP Paribas managers follow the Group’s Management Charter, which emphasises a positive, empowering team culture, encouraging open communication and supporting development and wellbeing. **Compliance Culture** We believe in promoting clear rules to foster a strong culture of compliance and ethics. We would expect any incoming member of staff to respect all financial security related legislation, including anti\-money laundering (AML); countering the financing of terrorism (CFT); international financial sanctions and embargoes (IFS); anti\-bribery and corruption (ABC); KYC; ongoing client related data screening; and transaction monitoring. BNP Paribas have clear policies on these topics which all staff are expected to familiarise themselves with upon successful entry to the Group. Should you require any reasonable accommodation for the interview process, please let us know. By submitting your application, you agree to BNP Paribas completing a background check and screening in advance of any potential offer being made.

title: Forward Deployed Solution Engineer – Applied AI FDE
description: **Company Description** It all started in sunny San Diego, California in 2004 when a visionary engineer, Fred Luddy, saw the potential to transform how we work. Fast forward to today — ServiceNow stands as a global market leader, bringing innovative AI\-enhanced technology to over 8,100 customers, including 85% of the Fortune 500®. Our intelligent cloud\-based platform seamlessly connects people, systems, and processes to empower organizations to find smarter, faster, and better ways to work. But this is just the beginning of our journey. Join us as we pursue our purpose to make the world work better for everyone. **Job Description** **Team Bio:** **ServiceNow’s Applied AI Forward Deployed Engineering (FDE)** team is where bold ideas meet transformative action. We partner with our most strategic customers to shape the future of enterprise AI. Together, we identify high\-value opportunities, accelerate business outcomes, and build reusable AI\-native solutions that advance the Now AI Platform. **Our mission:** We partner deeply with our customers to build intelligent, scalable AI solutions that solve their most mission\-critical challenges. By embedding in real\-world complexity, we deliver fast, iterate with purpose, and transform every success into reusable patterns that accelerate transformation across the Now Platform and the broader enterprise. **Why This Role Matters** Enterprises are raising the bar. AI initiatives must deliver business value—not just promise potential. That means taking cutting\-edge LLM capabilities and turning them into resilient, secure, and scalable software. As a **Senior Forward Deployed Software Engineer (FDSE)** , you act as the CTO of the build—owning everything from backend services to LLM pipelines and front\-end integrations. You partner with customers in the field to design, implement, and deliver solution\-ready builds in agile sprints. Your software becomes the reference implementation for scalable GenAI in the enterprise. You codify patterns, shape internal tooling, and accelerate innovation—delivering systems that are battle\-tested in production and scalable across industries. **Who You Are** You are a systems\-minded, AI\-native engineer who ships real software. You own the full stack—and are equally motivated by elegant APIs, intuitive UIs, and scalable orchestration pipelines. You think like a product\-minded CTO, balancing creativity with pragmatism to deliver impact. You embed deeply with customer teams, diagnose root problems, and architect AI\-powered workflows that run at scale. You don’t just debug code—you debug systems, context, and customer pain points. **You Will** * Build solution\-ready LLM\-enabled applications that span backend logic, data orchestration, and front\-end UI * Operate in the field, working side\-by\-side with customers to adapt, deploy, and iterate in live environments * Codify reusable assets—libraries, prompts, scaffolds—to accelerate future engagements * Shape developer experience by sharing feedback with platform and product teams **What You’ll Do** * Deliver Production \- ready solution in agile end\-to\-end sprints * Engineer with versatility: APIs, orchestration pipelines, vector DBs, LLM frameworks, UI components * Operate with agility: integrate with legacy systems, navigate ambiguity, ship safely at speed * Codify patterns: build scaffolds, SDKs, and documentation to scale success across customers * Influence platform: inform product strategy through field\-tested insights and extensible code **What Success Looks Like** * Production\-grade delivery: Your solution builds consistently convert to scaled deployments in production environments * Reusable impact: You author libraries, prompts, and scaffolds that power multiple deployments and projects * Platform influence: Your work shapes internal tooling and is integrated into platform roadmap and primitives * Velocity and precision: You move fast without breaking things—shaping resilient, secure systems in high\-stakes contexts * Engineering leadership: You are trusted by architects, PMs, and customer teams to lead implementation from zero to one **Qualifications** **What You Bring:** * Experience: In leveraging or critically thinking about how to integrate AI into work processes, decision\-making, or problem\-solving. This may include using AI\-powered tools, automating workflows, analyzing AI\-driven insights, or exploring AI’s potential impact on the function or industry * Relevant Experience: 8\+ years of software engineering, including 2\+ years building systems in customer\-facing or embedded roles * System architecture: Proven ability to design and implement AI\-native software in production environments * Engineering depth: Strength in backend (Python, Node.js, Java), frontend (React, Angular), APIs (REST/GraphQL) * LLM tooling: Familiarity with LangChain, Semantic Kernel, prompt chaining, vector search, and context management * Performance \& observability: Skilled in debugging distributed systems, tuning for latency, and implementing monitoring * Platform mindset: Can contribute to shared SDKs and tools, raising engineering velocity for the whole org * Product sensibility: Prioritize for user value, MVP iteration, and long\-term scale * DevOps fluency: Experience deploying in AWS, Azure, or GCP with CI/CD, containers, and infra\-as\-code * Field readiness: Able to travel up to 30% to embed onsite and deliver where it matters **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience integrating AI into SaaS platforms like ServiceNow or Salesforce * Track record of production deployments in secure, regulated enterprise environments * Contributions to dev experience tooling, frameworks, or reusable AI scaffolds **Join us at the frontier of enterprise AI—where your code powers AI transformation, your systems go live in the real world, and your ideas shape how the future scales.** Additional Information **Work Personas** We approach our distributed world of work with flexibility and trust. Work personas (flexible, remote, or required in office) are categories that are assigned to ServiceNow employees depending on the nature of their work and their assigned work location. Learn more here. To determine eligibility for a work persona, ServiceNow may confirm the distance between your primary residence and the closest ServiceNow office using a third\-party service. **Equal Opportunity Employer** ServiceNow is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, sexual orientation, national origin or nationality, ancestry, age, disability, gender identity or expression, marital status, veteran status, or any other category protected by law. In addition, all qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with legal requirements. **Accommodations** We strive to create an accessible and inclusive experience for all candidates. If you require a reasonable accommodation to complete any part of the application process, or are unable to use this online application and need an alternative method to apply, please contact globaltalentss@servicenow.com for assistance. **Export Control Regulations** For positions requiring access to controlled technology subject to export control regulations, including the U.S. Export Administration Regulations (EAR), ServiceNow may be required to obtain export control approval from government authorities for certain individuals. All employment is contingent upon ServiceNow obtaining any export license or other approval that may be required by relevant export control authorities. From Fortune. ©2025 Fortune Media IP Limited. All rights reserved. Used under license.

title: Founding AE
description: **Founding AE – Hypergrowth SaaS Start\-up** Dublin \| 5 days on\-site \| Equity included We’re partnering with one of the fastest\-growing SaaS start\-up companies in Dublin right now. They're growing rapidly and are now looking to hire a Founding AE who wants to be part of something that is genuinely going to the moon. This isn’t a role for someone who wants to sit in a big, structured sales org. It’s for someone who wants autonomy, ownership, and the chance to be part of a company that could become a real success story in the Dublin tech scene over the next few years. **The role** * Own the full sales cycle from discovery through to close * Sell into mid\-market customers across multiple industries * Work closely with the founders and leadership team * Help shape the sales process as the company scales * Take full ownership of pipeline and performance * Operate in a fast\-moving, high\-autonomy environment **What they’re looking for (essential)** * 3–5 years of closing experience in a SaaS sales role * Currently or previously carrying a **7\-figure annual quota** * Ideally Start up/Scale up experience * Can commit to 5 days on site in Dublin City Centre * Someone who is comfortable working in a fast\-growth start\-up * Self\-sufficient, driven, and comfortable with ownership * High energy, ambitious, and wants to be part of a company that’s going places **Why this role stands out** * Join early in what is expected to be a **hypergrowth journey** * Real autonomy – not a heavily micromanaged environment * **Equity included** as part of the package * Opportunity to grow with the company as it scales * Be part of a company that is aiming to become one of the standout start\-ups in Dublin Contact james.slein@adamantinehq.com for more information.

title: Full Stack Software Engineer II - Guest Engagement
description: Toast creates technology to help restaurants and local businesses succeed in a digital world, helping business owners operate, increase sales, engage customers, and keep employees happy. We’re seeking a Software Engineer to work within our team focused on building impactful capabilities that empower restaurants to drive Guest engagement, retention and growth. The team will help shape the future of how restaurants use technology to connect with their guests \- increasingly through AI\-powered tools and experiences that drive automation, personalization and smarter decision\-making. You will be a key part of a high\-impact, high\-ownership engineering team solving complex challenges in a fast\-moving product space. Your role will balance technical excellence alongside a strong product mindset to solve for continuous growth within both the team and our product space. **A Day In The Life (Responsibilities)** * Deliver projects that deeply connect restaurants with their guests within a cross\-functional collaborative and supportive environment. * Design, build, deploy and maintain highly resilient and scalable systems critical to our customers’ success. * Collaborate with team members and other engineers across the company. * Monitor feature rollouts to ensure customer success, iterating and improving when necessary. * Advocate for best coding practices, raising the bar for yourself, your team and the company. * Leverage cutting edge AI tools to enhance your development workflow, improve velocity, and help pioneer new approaches to building \- contributing to a culture of innovation and productivity across the team. **What You'll Need To Thrive (Requirements)** * Driven by impact and solving for the customer * Proficient in Java, Kotlin, or similar object\-oriented languages * Experience with modern web technologies and UI frameworks (e.g., React, Javascript , TypeScript, REST, GraphQL) * Enjoys building well\-engineered, usable and accessible systems * Ability to break down problems and communicate solutions clearly throughout cross\-functional teams. * Values personal and team growth, balancing task completion with platform stability. **Our Tech Stack** * A core of Java \& Kotlin based backend services. * React SPAs leveraging Tailwind, GraphQL and Storybook. * Android app built in Java \& Kotlin running in the restaurant. * PostgreSQL, Dynamodb for persistence. * Pulsar, Camel and RabbitMQ for message\-based integrations. * And many more technologies that help us build a top class platform. * This role requires a minimum of two days per week in our Dublin office\*\* **\#BI\-Hybrid** **AI at Toast** At Toast, one of our company values is that we're hungry to build and learn. We believe learning new AI tools empowers us to build for our customers faster, more independently, and with higher quality. We provide these tools across all disciplines, from Engineering and Product to Sales and Support, and are inspired by how our Toasters are already driving real value with them. The people who thrive here are those who embrace changes that let us build more for our customers; it’s a core part of our culture. **Our Total Rewards Philosophy** We strive to provide competitive compensation and benefits programs that help to attract, retain, and motivate the best and brightest people in our industry. Our total rewards package goes beyond great earnings potential and provides the means to a healthy lifestyle with the flexibility to meet Toasters’ changing needs. Learn more about our benefits at https://careers.toasttab.com/toast\-benefits. **How Toast Uses AI In Its Hiring Process** Throughout the hiring process, our goal is to get to know you. We use AI tools to support our recruiters and interviewers with tasks like note\-taking, summarization, and documentation of interviews to ensure they can be fully focused on your conversation. All hiring decisions are made by people. To learn more: https://careers.toasttab.com/ai\-in\-hiring ****Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion is Baked into our Recipe for Success**** At Toast, our employees are our secret ingredient—when they thrive, we thrive. The restaurant industry is one of the most diverse, and we embrace that diversity with authenticity, inclusivity, respect, and humility. By embedding these principles into our culture and design, we create equitable opportunities for all and raise the bar in delivering exceptional experiences. **We Thrive Together** We embrace a hybrid work model that fosters in\-person collaboration while valuing individual needs. Our goal is to build a strong culture of connection as we work together to empower the restaurant community. To learn more about how we work globally and regionally, check out: https://careers.toasttab.com/locations\-toast. **Apply today!** Toast is committed to creating an accessible and inclusive hiring process. As part of this commitment, we strive to provide reasonable accommodations for persons with disabilities to enable them to access the hiring process. If you need an accommodation to access the job application or interview process, please contact candidateaccommodations@toasttab.com. - *For roles in the United States, it is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.*

title: Full-stack Engineer - .Net/Python/Angular/React
description: **We’re partnering with a global technology and data\-driven organisation to hire a Full Stack Developer for a growing engineering team based in Dublin.** **This is a permanent opportunity suited to someone who enjoys building end\-to\-end solutions, working closely with product and architecture teams, and contributing to modern, AI\-enabled platforms in a collaborative environment.** **The role will focus on developing scalable enterprise applications, improving user workflows, and delivering secure, production\-grade software across both backend and frontend systems. You’ll work as part of a cross\-functional team with strong exposure to cloud technologies, modern UI development, API integrations, and emerging AI capabilities.** **Responsibilities** * Design, build, and maintain modern full stack applications across backend services, APIs, integrations, and frontend interfaces. * Work closely with Product Owners, Architects, and Business Analysts to translate business requirements into scalable technical solutions. * Develop responsive user interfaces using modern JavaScript frameworks such as Angular or React. * Build and enhance backend services using .NET, Entity Framework, and/or Python. * Contribute to integrations with AI and automation services, including API\-driven AI features and intelligent workflow capabilities. * Support CI/CD pipelines, deployment processes, and engineering best practices across the software development lifecycle. * Troubleshoot production issues, perform root cause analysis, and contribute to continuous improvement initiatives. * Collaborate with QA and performance teams to ensure applications are secure, testable, and scalable. * Provide mentorship and technical guidance to junior engineers within the team. **Requirements** * 4–7 years’ experience in software engineering or full stack development within enterprise environments. * Strong backend development experience with .NET / Entity Framework and/or Python. * Experience building modern frontend applications using Angular or React. * Good understanding of APIs, integrations, and scalable application design. * Exposure to cloud platforms, DevOps tooling, and CI/CD environments. * Experience working on workflow\-driven, B2B, or enterprise platforms would be beneficial. * Understanding of AI/ML/LLM integrations or experience consuming AI services via APIs is advantageous. * Strong communication skills with the ability to work effectively across distributed international teams. * Comfortable working independently and taking ownership of features from design through to deployment. **What’s On Offer** * €70\-74K and bonus package. * Private healthcare, pension, life assurance, and income protection benefits. * Flexible hybrid working model based in Dublin. * Opportunity to work on modern AI\-enabled products and enterprise\-scale platforms. * Strong focus on learning, career progression, and technical development. * Collaborative engineering culture with exposure to global teams and large\-scale transformation programmes. * Applicants must already hold the right to work in the Republic of Ireland.

title: General Practitioner - Laya Healthcare Clinics Dublin (Full-Time)
description: **Locations** Cherrywood \& Swords, Dublin **Working Days** Cherrywood: Monday, Tuesday \& Thursday \| Swords: Wednesday and Friday \| **Hours** 10 sessions per week **Salary** Competitive – dependent on experience **Contract Type** Permanent \| Full\-Time across both sites or Part\-Time/Job\-Share considered in each site **Reports To** Chief Medical Officer **About the Partnership** Laya Healthcare and WebDoctor have partnered to deliver an innovative healthcare model that combines the reach of digital consultations with the depth of in\-person clinical care. Operating across two fully equipped clinic locations in Cherrywood and Swords, this partnership is redefining how private healthcare is delivered in Ireland. We are seeking an experienced Lead General Practitioner to join this exciting venture and play a central role in delivering high quality patient care across both clinic sites. **The Opportunity** This is a unique opportunity to join a forward\-thinking healthcare company at an exciting stage of growth. As a GP in our clinics, you can deliver high\-quality consultations to patients presenting with a wide variety of conditions, supported by a dedicated Practice Nurse, Practice Manager, and integrated clinical systems. **Key Responsibilities** LAYA and Webdoctor are dedicated to delivering integrated, patient\-centric primary care through our innovative care model. As Lead General Practitioner across our new clinics in Cherrywood and Swords, you will be instrumental in bridging the gap between cutting\-edge digital health services and high\-quality, in\-person clinical excellence. This role requires a commitment to comprehensive care, ensuring that every patient benefits from seamless continuity whether they are engaging remotely or in the clinics, supported by modern technology and dedicated clinical staff. **Clinical Care** * Provide core general medical services to patients within the safety limitations of the clinic environment. * Have input into and oversight of specialist clinical services as appropriate and agreed. * We welcome GPs who are keen to explore and develop specialist service areas within the clinics, contributing to the expansion of our clinical offering. * Video Consultations: Embrace and utilise Webdoctor's digital\-first approach by providing high\-quality, remote video consultations alongside in\-person care, ensuring seamless continuity of service for all patients. * Collaborate with the Practice Nurse and wider clinical team to manage chronic disease and implement preventative health strategies. **Clinical Leadership \& Quality** * The Lead GP will take on a pivotal clinical leadership role within the clinics, working closely with the Chief Medical Officer to maintain high clinical standards, ensure quality patient care, and support the growth of the service. **Clinical Governance \& Quality Oversight** * Take responsibility for leading and implementing the clinical governance agenda within the clinics, including the development and regular review of clinical protocols, performance data, and patient safety incidents (e.g., significant event analysis and complaints review). * Ensure adherence to all medical protocols, clinic policies, and clinical governance standards. * Participate in team meetings, clinical audits, and quality improvement initiatives. * Complete patient records accurately and promptly using the clinic’s electronic medical record system. **Team Collaboration \& Mentorship** * Work collaboratively with the Practice Nurse, Practice Manager, and wider clinical team to ensure efficient, high\-quality patient care. * Communicate effectively with patients, providing clear and empathetic explanations of conditions and treatment options. * Oversee the training and onboarding process for new clinical staff, including GPs, Practice Nurses, and other healthcare professionals. * Act as a mentor and guide to less experienced clinical team members, fostering a culture of continuous professional development and high clinical standards. * Provide clinical supervision, advice, and professional support to other GPs and allied healthcare professionals within the Webdoctor service. * Conduct staff onboarding, training, and performance appraisals. * Work with the digital healthcare team to develop and manage hybrid healthcare services, where appropriate. **Service Development \& Management Interface** * Actively contribute clinical expertise to the strategic development of new services and clinical pathways, ensuring innovative offerings meet regulatory requirements and patient needs. * Support the phased rollout of new clinical services as the clinics grow. * Serve as the primary clinical interface for the Practice Manager and operational team on day\-to\-day clinical issues, ensuring seamless integration between clinical service delivery and operational efficiency. **Person Specification** **Essential** * Specialist registration with the Irish Medical Council (IMC) * Experience in general practice – minimum 3 years post\-specialist training preferred * Strong clinical acumen across a broad range of presentations * Excellent communication skills with a compassionate, patient\-focused approach * Ability to work both independently and collaboratively within a multidisciplinary team * Up\-to\-date knowledge of medical best practices, national guidelines, and relevant legislation * Right to work in Ireland * Ability to mentor and guide junior clinical staff, contributing to a culture of continuous learning and high clinical standards * Experience in clinical governance processes, including leading clinical audits, reviewing significant events, and contributing to quality improvement initiatives * Demonstrable management and leadership skills, with experience in or willingness to take responsibility for operational clinical oversight and team coordination **Desirable** * Experience in or willingness to develop skills in specialist areas * Experience with digital health platforms * Interest in contributing to service development and clinical innovation **What We Offer** * Competitive salary based on experience with transparent progression * Structured working hours with no on\-call commitments * Pension contribution of 3% employer contribution after 6 months * Discounted Laya Health and Dental Insurance * Bike to work scheme * Travel Tax Saver * A modern, fully equipped clinic environment * Supportive, collaborative team with dedicated Practice Nurse and Practice Manager * Opportunities for Continuing Professional Development (CPD) and specialist training which will be influenced by service needs. * A genuine opportunity to shape the growth of a new clinic and influence service delivery **How to Apply** If you are a skilled and motivated GP looking for a rewarding role in a modern, patient\-centred clinic environment, we would love to hear from you. Please submit your CV and a brief cover letter outlining your experience, areas of clinical interest, and suitability for the role.
