patients, providing clear and empathetic explanations of conditions and treatment options. * Oversee the training and onboarding process for new clinical staff, including GPs, Practice Nurses, and other healthcare professionals. * Act as a mentor and guide to less experienced clinical team members, fostering a culture of continuous professional development and high clinical standards. * Provide clinical supervision, advice, and professional support to other GPs and allied healthcare professionals within the Webdoctor service. * Conduct staff onboarding, training, and performance appraisals. * Work with the digital healthcare team to develop and manage hybrid healthcare services, where appropriate. **Service Development \& Management Interface** * Actively contribute clinical expertise to the strategic development of new services and clinical pathways, ensuring innovative offerings meet regulatory requirements and patient needs. * Support the phased rollout of new clinical services as the clinic grows. * Serve as the primary clinical interface for the Practice Manager and operational team on day\-to\-day clinical issues, ensuring seamless integration between clinical service delivery and operational efficiency. **Person Specification** **Essential** * Specialist registration with the Irish Medical Council (IMC) * Experience in general practice – minimum 3 years post\-specialist training preferred * Strong clinical acumen across a broad range of presentations * Excellent communication skills with a compassionate, patient\-focused approach * Ability to work both independently and collaboratively within a multidisciplinary team * Up\-to\-date knowledge of medical best practices, national guidelines, and relevant legislation * Right to work in Ireland * Ability to mentor and guide junior clinical staff, contributing to a culture of continuous learning and high clinical standards * Experience in clinical governance processes, including leading clinical audits, reviewing significant events, and contributing to quality improvement initiatives * Demonstrable management and leadership skills, with experience in or willingness to take responsibility for operational clinical oversight and team coordination **Desirable** * Experience in or willingness to develop skills in specialist areas * Experience with digital health platforms * Interest in contributing to service development and clinical innovation **What We Offer** * Competitive salary based on experience with transparent progression * Structured working hours with no on\-call commitments * Pension contribution of 3% employer contribution after 6 months * Discounted Laya Health and Dental Insurance * Bike to work scheme * Travel Tax Saver * A modern, fully equipped clinic environmentSupportive, collaborative team with dedicated Practice Nurse and Practice Manager * Opportunities for Continuing Professional Development (CPD) and specialist training which will be influenced by service needs. * A genuine opportunity to shape the growth of new clinics and influence service delivery **How to Apply** If you are a skilled and motivated GP looking for a rewarding role in a modern, patient\-centred clinic environment, we would love to hear from you. Please submit your CV and a brief cover letter outlining your experience, areas of clinical interest, and suitability for the role.

title: Graduate - Lending Operations (LAC) - based in Luxembourg
description: **This position is based at our Luxembourg headquarters and requires regular office presence** . The EIB offers you the opportunity to live and work in a truly international and multi\-cultural environment. We also offer relocation support. The EIB, the European Union's bank, in the framework of its GRAD programme, is seeking to recruit for its EIB Global Directorate (GLO), International Partners Department (IP), Latin America and Caribbean \- Public Sector Division (LAC), at its headquarters in Luxembourg, a **Graduate** **\- Lending Operations (LAC) \*** . **This is a full\-time temporary position at grade Graduate.** **The term of this contract will be 2 years.** * internal benchmark Graduate *Panel interviews are anticipated for end of June/early July.* Purpose In this role you will contribute to day\-to\-day operations and tasks, including the appraisal of new operations especially in the public sector as well as implementation and monitoring of existing operations and preparation of technical assistance and investment grant applications in support of the EIB operations in Latin America and the Caribbean. Operating Network You will report to the Head of Division and will, while working closely with Loan Officers in the Unit/ Division also have regular interaction with other colleagues in the Division/Department. You will also have regular contact with colleagues in other Directorates, notably, the Projects Directorate, Risk and Compliance Directorate, Legal Directorate, and Portfolio Management and Monitoring Directorate. You will also externally support in maintaining relations with public sector clients and partners, including International Financial Institutions, the European Commission, EU Delegations in partner countries, when required. Accountabilities *Under the supervision of the Head of Division, or other senior colleagues;* * Contribute to the operational lending matters, especially in the public sector. * Participate in the appraisal of operations and in preparation of the relevant documentation. * Contribute to the development of technical assistance programmes and relevant activities directly related to lending operations. * If applicable, participate in contract negotiations. * Assume responsibility for related internal and external reporting obligations and briefings. * Participate in transversal working groups, on an as needed basis. * Follow\-up and further develop relations with multi\-lateral, regional and bi\-lateral financial institutions in close coordination with loan officers. * Ensure correct input of data relating to own area of activity into the management information system and other relevant reporting areas. *Briefings, events and communication;* * Prepare, coordinate and consolidate inputs to presentations, data reports and briefings (often ad\-hoc) for events and other related activities (conferences, high\-level visits, etc.). * Contribute to external communications such as press releases, speeches, external reports etc. about the area of activity. **Qualifications** * University degree (minimum an equivalent to a Bachelor) preferably in Economy, Finance or a related field. * A maximum 2 years of professional experience after your most recent graduation (excluding summer jobs and internships shorter than nine months). * Some relevant experience in financial analysis, ideally in a financial institution or an international organization, is an asset. * Knowledge of lending operations and/or the Latin America and Caribbean (LAC) market, gained through academic or professional experience, is a distinct advantage. * Excellent drafting skills, analytical and synthesis skills. * Good inter\-personal, communication and representational/diplomatic skills, together with a strong team working spirit. * Proficiency in MS Office tools (Word, Excel, PowerPoint). * Excellent knowledge of English and/or French (\*\*\*), with a good command of the other. Additional moderate knowledge of written and spoken Portuguese or Spanish is required. Competencies **Find Out More About EIB Core Behavioural Skills Here** To find out more about our eligibility criteria click here *(\*\*\*) Unless stated explicitly as a required qualification, a good command of French is not a pre\-requisite for hire. As both English and French are however official working languages of the EIB, proficiency in both languages is a pre\-requisite for your future career development. Any language clause in your contract must be fulfilled in order for you to be eligible for a promotion (either via the annual appraisal cycle or via an internal selection process). Proficiency is understood to mean the attainment of level 5 of the Inter Institutional language courses, corresponding to B1\.2 of the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFRL). The Bank offers appropriate training support.* We hire and value talent with unique characteristics, creating a work environment where they can be themselves. We believe that Diversity, Equity and Inclusion makes us a performing and innovative organisation. We encourage all suitably qualified and eligible candidates to apply regardless of their gender identity/expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity. If you require reasonable accommodation during the recruitment process due to a disability, neurodivergence, or a chronic health condition, please contact the EIB Recruitment team Jobs@eib.org who will manage your request appropriately. By applying for this position, you acknowledge the importance of maintaining the security and integrity of the Information of the EIB Group. In case of selection for the position you agree to comply with all measures (policies, controls, document classification and management) implemented by the EIB Group to prevent unauthorised disclosure of any information or any damage to the EIB Group reputation. Deadline for applications: **22nd May 2026**

title: Graduate Management Trainee - Dublin
description: **Overview** We’re Enterprise Mobility. A family\-owned, global mobility leader with a $39 billion turnover, nearly 90,000 team members, and operations in 95 countries. Led by CEO Chrissy Taylor, the third generation of the Taylor family, we’re built on a legacy that gives us the stability to focus on the long\-term success of our people, our customers and our business. **Why join the Graduate Management Training Programme?** As a Graduate Management Trainee, you'll have the freedom and support to explore your leadership potential \- and the opportunity to become a branch manager in one of our retail operations in as little as two years. In fact, most of our senior leaders began their careers in this very role \- including our current CEO. From day one, we’ll invest in you. You’ll be in a supportive environment where you’ll take on real responsibilities and gain invaluable hands\-on experience in customer service, sales, marketing, finance, operations, and more. **We work hard and reward hard work** Your work will be recognised with performance\-based incentives and opportunities for continued promotion. Our unique promote\-from\-within culture means you can keep your career moving forward without having to change organisations. **Award\-winning training and development** Whether you’re building on existing strengths or developing new ones, your growth is our priority. Through classroom learning, on\-the\-job training, and mentorship, you’ll have the tools and support to take the next step – and the one after that. **Our doors are open** As a Graduate Management Trainee, your experience at Enterprise Mobility will be as unique as you because you shape who we are as much as we shape your skills, path and potential. We pride ourselves on opening our doors to a variety of voices, giving way to an experience that’s both inclusive and that inspires diversity of thought. **Responsibilities** From your very first day, you’ll be trusted with real responsibility and exposed to all areas of our business. You’ll develop skills in: **Customer Service:** deliver exceptional experiences by confidently handling reservations, resolving enquiries, and building rapport with a diverse range of customers. **Sales and Marketing:** connect with local business partners, grow your network and develop lasting relationships. **Financial Control and Profitability:** understand the financial mechanics of a successful business, including cost control and interpreting profit and loss statements. **Operations and Logistics:** learn how to manage a fleet, plan strategically, and drive performance. **Leadership and Development:** take the lead in mentoring, training, and developing your own team \- with the opportunity to manage and promote others as you grow. **Qualifications** * A Bachelor’s degree is preferred; however, professional experience can be substituted if applicable. * You must have a full manual Republic of Ireland, UK or EU driving licence, but we do make accommodations for applicants who don’t drive due to a disability. * No drug or alcohol related offence on driving record within the last five years is permitted. **Additional Information** * In accordance with current employment permit regulations, we are unable to support an employment permit application for this role, as it is not eligible under the current system. * Regardless of your socio\-economic background, university attended, subject studied, and degree attained, we will always look at how you perform against our competencies and will judge you on that alone. * Please let us know about any accommodations you may need to participate in our recruitment process. * Please limit your application to only one job posting based on where you live and/or plan to work. Applying to multiple locations will delay your application being processed. * This job posting is for applications within the following location(s): Dublin

title: Graduate Program - Process & Operations Transformation
description: This role has been designed as ‘Hybrid’ with an expectation that you will work on average 2 days per week from an HPE office. **Who We Are** Hewlett Packard Enterprise is the global edge\-to\-cloud company advancing the way people live and work. We help companies connect, protect, analyze, and act on their data and applications wherever they live, from edge to cloud, so they can turn insights into outcomes at the speed required to thrive in today’s complex world. Our culture thrives on finding new and better ways to accelerate what’s next. We know varied backgrounds are valued and succeed here. We have the flexibility to manage our work and personal needs. We make bold moves, together, and are a force for good. If you are looking to stretch and grow your career our culture will embrace you. Open up opportunities with HPE. **Job Description** **About the Program** As a rotational Graduate in our **PREP (Professional Readiness \& Excellence Program)** within the **Office of the Chief Operating Officer (OCOO),** you will work on strategic, operational, and transformational initiatives across multiple OCOO teams for **12\-24 months in Ireland** . **Starting in September 2026,** you will gain hands\-on experience in service excellence, operations transformation, digital strategy, process engineering, customer delivery, financial analysis, insight generation, and executive\-level program management. **PREPs are placed in high‑impact teams led by OCOO Leadership Team members, including:** * Process \& Operations Transformation (PRIMARY) * Digital Strategy * OCOO Strategy \& Operations * Service Excellence APJ, NA, LATAM, EMEA * Service Excellence Embedded Financing This program aims to attract, develop, and retain early‑career talent, preparing them to become the next generation of OCOO leaders. **Program Structure** You will be part of a global PREP cohort with: * Formal onboarding * Assigned mentors * Soft‑skills and leadership training * Monthly check‑ins and development assessments * End\-of\-project reporting and executive presentations You will participate in rotations across different teams in the OCOO organization, being assigned to specific projects with goal setting and clear metrics of accomplishments through the duration of your stay. Upon graduation after the 1–2\-year program, you would be a candidate to be assigned to a final placement based on your performance, skills developed, profile preference, and business needs. This end‑to‑end structure differentiates PREP as a strategic talent pipeline for OCOO. In this position your focus area will be **Process \& Operations Transformation.** **Required Qualifications** * Recent graduate (Bachelor’s or Master’s) in Business, Finance, Engineering, Data Analytics, Supply Chain, IT, or related fields. * Valid work permission for Ireland * Ability to work with cross\-functional teams across global time zones. * Demonstrated leadership capability through internships, student leadership, or project work. * High learning agility, self\-drive, and adaptability. * Outstanding written \& verbal English communication **Preferred Qualifications And Competencies** * Experience with process analysis, financing operations, digital tools, workflow mapping, or automation. * Internship experience in financial institutions, operations, strategy, digital, or customer‑facing roles (strongly preferred, given PREP’s rotation opportunities). * Strategic thinking \& systems understanding * Business acumen and data\-driven decision making * Customer\-centric mindset * Adaptability \& comfort navigating ambiguity * Professional maturity and strong relationship\-building skills **What You Can Expect From Us** * Initial extensive onboarding to support you with adjusting to each role and team * Ongoing learning and development throughout the program * Competitive salary and great benefits * Great work\-life balance, including hybrid working and Wellness Fridays initiative **Ready to take the next step?** Open up opportunities with HPE. **Additional Skills** Accountability, Accountability, Action Planning, Active Learning, Active Listening, Bias, Business, Calendar Management, Coaching, Computer Literacy, Creativity, Critical Thinking, Design Thinking, Empathy, Follow\-Through, Group Problem Solving, Growth Mindset, Intellectual Curiosity (Inactive), Long Term Planning, Managing Ambiguity, Office Administration, Policy and procedures, Recordkeeping, Risk Assessment, Software Development {\+ 5 more} **What We Can Offer You** **Health \& Wellbeing** We strive to provide our team members and their loved ones with a comprehensive suite of benefits that supports their physical, financial and emotional wellbeing. **Personal \& Professional Development** We also invest in your career because the better you are, the better we all are. We have specific programs catered to helping you reach any career goals you have — whether you want to become a knowledge expert in your field or apply your skills to another division. **Unconditional Inclusion** We are unconditionally inclusive in the way we work and celebrate individual uniqueness. We know varied backgrounds are valued and succeed here. We have the flexibility to manage our work and personal needs. We make bold moves, together, and are a force for good. **Let's Stay Connected** Follow @HPECareers on Instagram to see the latest on people, culture and tech at HPE. \#ireland \#grads **Job** Administration **Job Level** Entry HPE is an Equal Employment Opportunity/ Veterans/Disabled/LGBT employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, gender, or any other protected category, and all decisions we make are made on the basis of qualifications, merit, and business need. Our goal is to be one global team that is representative of our customers, in an inclusive environment where we can continue to innovate and grow together. Please click here: Equal Employment Opportunity. **Hewlett Packard Enterprise is EEO Protected Veteran/ Individual with Disabilities.** HPE will comply with all applicable laws related to employer use of arrest and conviction records, including laws requiring employers to consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories. **No Fees Notice \& Recruitment Fraud Disclaimer** *It has come to HPE’s attention that there has been an increase in recruitment fraud whereby scammer impersonate HPE or HPE\-authorized recruiting agencies and offer fake employment opportunities to candidates. These scammers often seek to obtain personal information or money from candidates.* *Please note that Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE), its direct and indirect subsidiaries and affiliated companies, and its authorized recruitment agencies/vendors **will never charge any candidate a registration fee, hiring fee, or any other fee in connection with its recruitment and hiring process.** The credentials of any hiring agency that claims to be working with HPE for recruitment of talent should be verified by candidates and candidates shall be solely responsible to conduct such verification. Any candidate/individual who relies on the erroneous representations made by fraudulent employment agencies does so at their own risk, and HPE disclaims liability for any damages or claims that may result from any such communication.*

title: Graduate Surveyor (Cork)
description: **Who Are We** Founded in 1896, Knight Frank was registered as Knight Frank LLP a Limited Liability Partnership, on 3 November 2003, registered in England \& Wales at 55 Baker Street, London, W1U 8AN with the registered number of OC305934\. Headquartered in London, UK, Knight Frank is one of the world's leading independent real estate consultancies. We work responsibly in partnership to enhance people's lives and environments ***in over 50 Markets, 600\+ Offices, 20,000\+ People, 1 Global Network.*** At the heart of all we do are our Clients. A relationship built on trust is vital. As trusted partners in property, we act with integrity and care, understanding our clients’ unique needs and consistently thinking about the bigger picture to provide personalised, clear and considered advice across all areas of property. Through our deep understanding of key markets and sectors – both emerging and established – we are dedicated to meeting and exceeding their property goals. A true partnership is a balanced one, and our People are passionate about, and committed to, making our business inclusive and diverse. We give every individual the space and opportunity to perform at their best and be recognised for the immense value they bring. We empower everyone to have autonomy in their role and encourage them to use their voice in how we can make a positive impact as a firm and shape the future of real estate, the built environment and the impact on the communities we serve. Our collaborative and engaged teams provide excellent and dedicated client service. In our workplace, opinions are respected, everyone is invited to contribute to the success of our business and innovation, and new ideas are celebrated. In 2021 we celebrated our 125th anniversary and set out Our Desired Future, cemented in three pillars: People Potential, Client Centricity and always Creating our Future, together. Learn more about what sets us apart here. **About The Role** Knight Frank is seeking a motivated and ambitious Graduate Surveyor to join our Commercial team in Cork. This role offers an excellent opportunity to develop your career within a dynamic and supportive environment. You will gain exposure to a broad range of commercial property activities while working alongside experienced professionals. Full support, guidance, and resources will be provided to help you successfully pursue and achieve your APC qualification. **Responsibilities** * Assist with valuation instructions and inspections for all property sectors including investment, development land, office, industrial and retail for the purposes of secured lending and fund valuations. * Contribute to the delivery of all projects to include inspections, surveys, researching, drafting reports and valuations, inputting comparable information into our database. * Assist with agency instructions to include the sale and leasing of all types of commercial property. * Assist with the marketing of properties and pitches. * Assist with lease advisory instructions to include rent reviews and lease renewals. * Maintain and update databases, ensure timely responses and that all details are recorded accurately on the relevant system. * Utilise all relevant technology systems provided, maintain up to date and accurate records, ensuring valuations are continually and appropriately recorded. * Collaborate with the wider Commercial team, contributing to the delivery of the business plan, while supporting each other in the achievement of KPIs. * Identify and act on referrals as they present themselves across Knight Frank. * Live the Company Values in all dealings with colleagues and take responsibility for own development by undertaking activities to broaden experience and market knowledge, while also sharing your expertise and providing coaching and feedback to others. * Support the enhancement of the Knight Frank brand. * Develop client relationships and seek to build on our client base. **Requirements** * SCSI accredited degree. * Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, ability to multitask and meet deadlines. * Proficient in MS packages. Knowledge of relevant IT packages such as Argus would be an advantage. * Excellent report writing and numeracy skills. * PSRA Licence. * Full clean driver licence. Competitive salary Please note: this is a Direct Search led by Knight Frank. Applications from recruitment agencies will not be accepted nor will fees be paid for unsolicited CVs, even if provided by PSL agencies.

title: Graduate Sustainability Engineer
description: **About The Role – Graduate Sustainability Engineer** As a Graduate Sustainability Engineer at Ethos Engineering, you will play a crucial role within our Sustainability Group, which focuses on advancing environmentally responsible building practices and certifications. You will have the opportunity to work on exciting projects and contribute to the design and implementation of sustainable building systems, including involvement with green building certifications such as HPI and LEED. **Candidate Attributes** * Candidate must be based in Ireland to be considered for this position. * Bachelor’s degree in Engineering/ Environmental/ Sustainability, Building Service, Architecture or a related field. * A strong interest in sustainability and green building practices. * Excellent problem\-solving skills and attention to detail. * Strong communication and teamwork abilities. * Knowledge of sustainability certifications, particularly HPI and LEED, is a plus. * Familiarity with sustainability assessment tools and software is desirable. **Role Requirements** * Collaborate with the Sustainability Group to develop and implement sustainable design strategies in building projects. * Gain hands\-on experience with certifications such as HPI (Home Performance Index) and LEED (Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design). * Contribute to the assessment of building energy performance and recommend energy\-saving solutions. * Stay up\-to\-date with industry trends and best practices related to sustainable design and green building certifications. * Analyse data related to sustainability metrics, energy consumption, and environmental impact, providing insights to enhance project sustainability. * Collaborate with clients to understand their sustainability goals and help translate them into actionable plans. * Assist in the preparation of reports, presentations, and documentation required for green building certification submissions. * Work closely with multidisciplinary project teams to integrate sustainable design principles into engineering solutions. **Why Join Us?** At Ethos, we’re dedicated to fostering an environment that encourages both **technical excellence and personal wellbeing** . **We offer:** * Flexible Fridays and a 9\-day fortnight working schedule — enjoy every second Friday off to maintain work–life balance. * A culture that values flexibility, learning, and teamwork, with clear progression pathways and support toward professional accreditation. * Opportunities to work alongside some of the most experienced engineers in the industry, within a forward\-thinking, technology\-driven consultancy. **Benefits:** * Flexible working times / Hybrid Working * 20 days of holidays, increasing up to 25 days * Pension Scheme * Your own company laptop * Coverage for working travel out\-of\-pocket expenses * Travel tax scheme or bike\-to\-work scheme * Regular training, mentoring, and further college support * Support for Chartership * A professional subscription fee paid by the company * Heavily subsidized social committee activities (5\-a\-side football, golf society, climbing club, and regular social events) Here at Ethos we believe in equal opportunities and that each individual can bring something ‘WoW’ to our organisation. So if you don't necessarily meet every single point on the job description, but think you have what it takes, please still get in touch. We'd love to have a chat and see if you could be a great fit. **At Ethos, our *vision* is to:** ***“Be the company people want to work with, and for”*** **Values** Our values differentiate and focus us every day to be **EPIC** \- for our people and our customers. Recruitment Agencies: Please do not reply to this job advert. Should Ethos require assistance with this posting you will be contacted by our Talent Acquisition Team.

title: Guest Experience Supervisor

Set up accurate accounts for each guest according to their requirements. Enter Marriott Rewards information. Ensure rates match market codes, document exceptions. Secure payment prior to issuing room key, verify/adjust billing. Compile and review daily reports/logs/contingency lists. Complete cashier and closing reports. Supply guests with directions and property information. Accommodate guest requests, contacting appropriate staff if necessary. Follow up to ensure requests have been met. Process all payment types, vouchers, paid\-outs, and charges. Balance and drop receipts. Count and secure bank at beginning and end of shift. Enter and locate information using computers/POS systems. Stand, sit, or walk for an extended period of time. PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS Education: High school diploma or G.E.D. equivalent. Related Work Experience: At least 1 year of related work experience. Supervisory Experience: At least 1 year of supervisory experience. License or Certification: None *At Marriott International, we are dedicated to being an equal opportunity employer, welcoming all and providing access to opportunity. We actively foster an environment where the unique backgrounds of our associates are valued and celebrated. Our greatest strength lies in the rich blend of culture, talent, and experiences of our associates. We are committed to non\-discrimination on any protected basis, including disability, veteran status, or other basis protected by applicable law.* The Autograph Collection is a growing collection of four and five\-star independent hotels, each offering unique and memorable experiences. In joining Autograph Collection, you join a portfolio of brands with Marriott International. **Be** where you can do your best work, **begin** your purpose, **belong** to an amazing global team, and **become** the best version of you.

title: Infrastructure & Capital Projects - Digital Construction (All Levels)
description: **Team:** Industry X, Infrastructure \& Capital Projects **Job Role:** Digital Construction (All Levels) **Location:** Dublin or Cork, Ireland (with expected travel) **As a team:** In Industry X we craft the future of making things, and the things we make. Our Industry X team drives innovation by delivering the best\-in\-class management consultancy based on industrial experience demonstrated by the latest technologies. Evolution of industry practices have been witnessed with evolution of technology. We've been helping businesses embrace and implement the new for years; it is crucial we apply this to manufacturers and products companies, service organizations and software companies, to fuel their next level of growth. Within **Infrastructure \& Capital Projects** , we shape how infrastructure is planned, delivered and operated. This is one of Accenture’s fastest‑growing global practices, strengthened by recent strategic acquisitions and significant investment. Our team combines **engineering, programme delivery and digital expertise** to help clients modernise how they deliver capital projects—using technologies such as **Building Information Modelling (BIM)** and **Virtual Design and Construction (VDC)** to improve outcomes across the full asset lifecycle. You’ll be joining at a pivotal moment of growth, working alongside experts across Industry X to digitise, optimise and transform traditional capital project delivery. The opportunity: Accenture is seeking **Digital Engineering practitioners** with strong **BIM delivery experience** to join our Infrastructure \& Capital Projects team in Ireland. This role focuses on defining BIM and VDC strategies and leading their implementation across **greenfield and brownfield construction and engineering programmes** , spanning design, construction and handover. **In this role you will:** - Drive digital transformation across capital projects, helping clients realise value through BIM and VDC‑enabled delivery models across the full asset lifecycle. - Define and govern BIM delivery, including developing BIM Guidelines, reviewing BIM Execution Plans, and ensuring alignment across design and construction teams. - Provide end‑to‑end BIM oversight, ensuring effective 3D design coordination, high‑quality model reviews, smooth handover from design to construction, and delivery of accurate as‑built models. - Design future ways of working for clients, shaping operating models, processes and technology solutions in collaboration with business, engineering and digital stakeholders. - Build trusted client relationships and support growth, contributing to innovation workshops, proposal development, sales pursuits, and thought leadership across the Industry X practice. **We are looking for:** - Experience delivering complex capital projects from a client‑facing programme or project delivery perspective, with accountability for time, cost and quality outcomes. - Strong BIM and VDC delivery expertise, including hands‑on experience managing BIM processes on commercial, institutional or infrastructure projects. - Proficiency with industry‑standard BIM platforms, such as Autodesk Construction Cloud (ACC), Navisworks, and Common Data Environment (CDE) solutions. - Sector experience in asset‑ or energy‑intensive industries, such as Utilities, Energy, Chemicals, Transport, Telecoms, Construction Services or Data Centres. - Ability to lead and collaborate effectively, working as a delivery lead or subject‑matter expert within multi‑disciplinary consulting and engineering teams. **Target industries are Asset\- and Energy\-intensive industries including:** - Energy, Utilities (water, power, nuclear), Chemicals - Infrastructure and Networks including Road \& Rail, Telecoms, Construction Services, Data Centres **Experience in one or more of the following would be an advantage:** - Delivery of data centre or other highly complex facilities - Engineering or science background, or equivalent practical experience - Programme, portfolio or development‑phase leadership on large‑scale schemes - Defining transformation roadmaps, solution architectures and operating models - Consulting delivery experience, including team leadership roles - Sales and business development support, including RFP responses and solution shaping - Large‑scale delivery and organisational change management - Established client relationships and industry network If you’re passionate about **digitising capital project delivery** , enjoy working across **engineering, technology and consulting** , and want to make a tangible impact at scale, this is an exciting opportunity to grow your career within Accenture’s Industry X practice. **Does this sond like you? Then we'd love to hear from you!** Please also note that Accenture is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all sections of society and does not discriminate on grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability, age, citizenship, marital, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation, or gender identity. Please advise if you have any particular requirements, so we can look into arranging reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process. You can contact us at accessIErecruitment@accenture.com

title: Interchange & Scheme Fees Analyst
description: At U.S. Bank, we’re on a journey to do our best. Helping the customers and businesses we serve to make better and smarter financial decisions, enabling the communities we support to grow and succeed in the right ways, all more confidently and more often—that’s what we call the courage to thrive. We believe it takes all of us to bring our shared ambition to life, and each person is unique in their potential. A career with U.S. Bank gives you a wide, ever\-growing range of opportunities to discover what makes you thrive. Try new things, learn new skills and discover what you excel at—all from Day One. As a wholly owned subsidiary of U.S. Bank, Elavon is committed to building the platforms and ecosystems that help over 1\.5 million customers around the world to achieve their financial goals—no matter what they need. From transaction processing to customer service, to driving innovation and launching new products, we’re building a range of tailored payment solutions powered by the latest technology. As part of our team, you can explore what motivates and energizes your career goals: partnering with our customers, our communities, and each other. We actively uphold transparent and fair hiring practices that support individual opportunity, inclusive culture, and career mobility across all levels of our organisation. We offer meaningful opportunities for growth, a culture of inclusion, and a strong commitment to transparency and integrity in everything we do. **Job Description** The Interchange and Scheme Fee Team is responsible for managing and analysing the complex fee structures associated with card transactions, primarily within the European market, but also across APAC, MEA, and LAC regions. Our team ensures accurate calculation, reporting, and optimization of interchange and scheme fees across various payment networks. **Key Accountabilities** * Interchange and / or Scheme Fees management (including but not limited to bulletins analyses, tracking changes, products implementation, rates management, cost reconciliation, and procedures maintenance) * Conducts comprehensive financial analyses, including budgeting, forecasting, expense allocation, ad hoc business case development, peer group comparisons, and internal consulting across all management levels. * Develops and maintains advanced spreadsheets and statistical models * Prepares a wide range of management reports and presentations to communicate financial insights effectively. * Investigates and resolves discrepancies or errors in financial data sourced from reporting systems. * Assists in the development and enhancement of financial applications and tools. **Skills \& experience required:** * Working knowledge of financial analysis techniques * Well\-developed mathematical and analytical skills * Ability to identify and resolve exceptions * Strong technical skills related to data mining and visualization tools * Advanced MS Excel * Experience with SQL or a commitment to learning it within the first 3 months * Ability to read and comprehend technical documentation from card brands (e.g., Visa, Mastercard, JCB, AMEX, and others), published in English * Additional information **Location expectations** This role requires working from a U.S. Bank location three (3\) or more days per week. **Accessibility** We are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment experience. If you need adjustments at any stage of the application or hiring process, please contact your recruiter for guidance and support. **Total Rewards** U.S. Bank is committed to fair, equitable, and transparent compensation practices in line with local regulatory and legal requirements. Our total rewards approach is designed to attract, retain, and support top talent while ensuring equal pay for work of equal value. We offer a market\-competitive compensation package that includes: * Clearly defined salary ranges aligned with industry benchmarks and internal equity standards. * Performance\-based incentives for eligible employees (as defined by relevant plan rules), awarded through transparent, objective criteria that recognize both individual and company performance. * Inclusive equitable benefits that are accessible to all employees and focused around our 3 main pillars of financial wellbeing, health \& wellness). * Continuous development opportunities including training, education support, and career progression pathways based on inclusive and transparent criteria. * Employee recognition programs that celebrate achievements and milestones for all. We regularly review our compensation and benefits to ensure they remain competitive, inclusive, and responsive to employee needs and market trends. Further details of the compensation package will be provided upon application. We encourage candidates to explore the full value of our offer, including monetary and non\-monetary benefits, at Employee benefits and development \| U.S. Bank \| Elavon. **Closing Date** Posting may be closed earlier due to high volume of applicants. We aim to provide timely updates throughout the process and encourage early applications to ensure consideration.

Follow us on LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, and YouTube to explore our AECOM voices, employee stories, latest projects, and much more! For further information about the role, reach out to the recruiter on LinkedIn Colum Calnan **About AECOM** AECOM is the world’s trusted infrastructure consulting firm, delivering professional services throughout the project lifecycle – from advisory, planning, design and engineering to program and construction management. On projects spanning transportation, buildings, water, new energy and the environment, our public\- and private\-sector clients trust us to solve their most complex challenges. Our teams are driven by a common purpose to deliver a better world through our unrivaled technical and digital expertise, a culture of equity, diversity and inclusion, and a commitment to environmental, social and governance priorities. AECOM is a Fortune 500 firm and its Professional Services business had revenue of $16\.1 billion in fiscal year 2025\. See how we are delivering sustainable legacies for generations to come at aecom.com and @AECOM. **Freedom to Grow in a World of Opportunity** You will have the flexibility you need to do your best work with hybrid work options. Whether you’re working from an AECOM office, remote location or at a client site, you will be working in a dynamic environment where your integrity, entrepreneurial spirit and pioneering mindset are championed. You will help us foster a safe and respectful workplace, where we invite everyone to bring their whole selves to work using their unique talents, backgrounds and expertise to create transformational outcomes for our clients. AECOM provides a wide array of compensation, benefits and well\-being programs to meet the diverse needs of our employees and their families. We’re the world’s trusted global infrastructure firm, and we’re in this together – your growth and success are ours too. Join us, and you’ll get all the benefits of being a part of a global, publicly traded firm – access to industry\-leading technology and thinking and transformational work with big impact and work flexibility. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we believe in each person’s potential, and we’ll help you reach yours. All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines. **ReqID:** REF46059N **Business Line:** PCC **Business Group:** DCS **Strategic Business Unit:** Europe \& India **Career Area:** Survey \& Estimation **Work Location Model:** Hybrid

title: Junior AV Engineer & Office Support
description: **Junior Audio Visual Engineer and Oﬃce Support** **About the Role:** Mood Media, one of the world's leading background music and digital signage providers, are looking to hire an apprentice AV engineer . The Junior Audio Visual Engineer and Office Support role is designed to provide essential technical assistance and operational support within a dynamic office environment in Ireland. The successful candidate will be responsible supporting the technical project management team, learning and ultimately responsible for small projects, working with the project managers to help specify equipment and generate hardware quotations, set up and install our music players when required. Assist the main engineer with the set up, cabling and installing of PA systems in retail stores and commercial display monitors and media players to create professional digital signage solution country wide, as and when required to do so. Predominately office based role. Working hours are typically 9am to 5:00pm Monday to Friday. Oﬃce Location is in Blackrock, Co. Dublin. Occasional Overtime and Overnights Pension and VHI Health Insurance **Minimum Qualifications:** * Experience in Audio Visual Technology, Electronics, IT, or a related technical field is an advantage * Basic understanding of audio\-visual systems including hardware and software components. * Experience with setting up and troubleshooting AV equipment in a professional environment. * Proficiency in using common office software such as Microsoft Office and Google packages. * Strong communication skills and the ability to work collaboratively within a team. * Having your own transport is preferred but not necessary. A full driving licence is essential. **Preferred Qualifications:** * Previous experience in an office support or technical support role. * Ability to work with computer assisted design software, Viso, Auto Cad Etc * LED systems knowledge preferable; * Knowledge of network fundamentals related to AV equipment connectivity. * Certification in AV technologies such as CTS (Certified Technology Specialist) is advantageous. * Experience with inventory management and vendor coordination. **Responsibilities:** * Working with the project management team to help specify equipment and generate hardware quotations for the sales team * Provide technical support to the Irish Office * Assist in the installation and configuration of new AV hardware and software, supporting the current engineer as and when required. * Set up and install our music players when required * Support general office operations such as managing inventory of AV supplies, coordinating with vendors, and assisting with administrative tasks as needed.

title: Junior Pricing Actuary
description: **Company Description** PartnerRe is a leading, privately owned, multi\-line global reinsurer with a reputation of financial stability and strength, and a commitment to rebuilding businesses and communities after risk events around the world. The role will provide the successful candidate with a broad range of actuarial and commercial skills with strong opportunities for future career progression. **Key Responsibilities** * Contribute to the growth of the UK \& Ireland Protection team * Price protection business efficiently and accurately * Liaise closely with client partners to meet commercial goals * Support the wider Key Accounts team on important projects **Qualifications** **Technical Skills:** * Strong analytical skills * Knowledge of R Coding or similar statistical package * Solid grasp of Actuarial Mathematics * Data validation skills **Behavioural Skills** * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills * Attention to Detail and Accuracy * Self\-motivated with the ability to manage time and multiple priorities effectively * High degree of accountability * Strong interpersonal skills * High degree of personal competence (self\-awareness, self\-management) * Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English (internal stakeholders), including the ability to communicate complex technical issues in an effective way **Work Experience** * 1\-3 years of relevant work experience **Education/Professional Qualifications** * Graduate of Actuarial Science, Mathematics or related discipline * Part Qualified Actuary studying towards an actuarial designation **Additional Information** : PartnerRe provides a working environment in which diversity is valued, where all employees feel welcome, respected, and empowered to perform at their best. We invite you to learn more about D\&I at PartnerRe. Thank you, PartnerRe Hiring Team

title: Junior Recruitment Consultant - No Experience Required
description: * **Transparent promotion pathway — you control how fast you progress** * **Uncapped commission — your results determine your income** * **Industry\-leading training — equips you with elite\-level expertise** At Archer, advancement is earned. We run a performance\-led environment with clearly defined goals, open expectations, and progression driven by results, not tenure or office politics. Strong performance doesn’t go unnoticed here; it’s recognised quickly and rewarded with accelerated career growth. High performers earn 3 promotions in their first year! We specialise in developing ambitious professionals into Recruitment and Business Development experts. You’ll benefit from practical, one\-on\-one coaching, a clear and structured progression framework, and a commission structure with no ceiling, expanding as you progress. Our consultants build genuine market expertise, unlock significant earning potential, and move forward in their careers far faster than in most fields. **Why Join the Team?** * **Drive Growth:** Meet clients across the IT market and help power our expansion with outstanding service and results. * **Make an Impact:** Play a key role in shaping our success while fast\-tracking your own career. * **Level Up Fast:** Get expert training and mentorship to grow into a top performer and future leader. * **Earn Without Limits:** Benefit from a strong base salary plus uncapped commission that truly rewards high achievers. **Your day\-to\-day as a Recruitment Consultant:** * **Client Management:** Build and maintain strong relationships with clients from a varied range of industries. * **Candidate Sourcing:** Proactively source, screen, and qualify candidates for various roles within the IT sector. Utilise a variety of sourcing methods, including LinkedIn, networking, and database management. * **Market Insight:** Stay informed about the IT industry, including trends, challenges, and opportunities. Use this knowledge to inform recruitment strategies and provide insights to clients and candidates. **What You Will Have:** * 3\+ years of professional experience (any industry can be considered) \- No recruitment experience required * A proactive, ambitious mindset and the confidence to take ownership of your growth * Strong communication skills and a natural drive to succeed **Benefits \& Perks** * Basic Salary \+ Uncapped Commission \+Additional Bonus Structure (Average OTE: Year 1 €48k\+, Year 2 €69k\+, Year 3 €100k\+) * Internal Learning and development, helping support and train alongside the support of your manager * Clear path for progression: 13 promotions available all the way to Equity Partner \+ Leadership Academy programme for managers in the making * Sabbatical: Paid sabbatical every 3 years * Industry\-leading training programme with 150\+ training modules * Fantastic team culture, very collaborative and regular team socials **Turn ambition into reward** At Archer, performance drives growth and uncapped earnings. Apply today and start building the career you deserve. Contact Ciara O’Flynn at **ciara.oflynn@archer.ie** or **\+353 1 902 6804\.**

title: Junior Telecoms Engineer
description: Yellowcom are a leading ROI \& UK mobile and landline provider, delivering services to businesses from our offices in Dublin, Belfast \& Glasgow for over 18 years. We are fully accredited partners with Fixed Line Operators, Mobile Networks and Telecom Manufacturers. We are looking for a Junior Engineer to join our growing Dublin team. This is an excellent opportunity for someone at the early stages of their career who is eager to develop hands\-on experience in telecoms and IT infrastructure. In this role, you will assist senior engineers with installations at customer sites, including structured cabling and telephone systems (both PBX and hosted). You will also support troubleshooting and maintenance tasks, helping to diagnose and resolve faults both onsite and remotely, while delivering excellent customer service. This position offers strong development opportunities, with training and exposure to the latest telecoms technologies. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist with installation of structured cabling and telecom systems * Support configuration of phone systems (on\-premise and cloud\-based) * Help diagnose and resolve faults onsite and remotely * Work alongside senior engineers on customer projects * Maintain clear communication with customers and internal teams * Ensure all work is completed to a high standard **The Ideal Candidate Will Have** * Basic knowledge or interest in structured cabling or telecoms systems * Understanding of networking fundamentals (routers, LANs, IP basics) * Strong willingness to learn and develop new technical skills * Good problem\-solving ability and attention to detail * Strong communication and interpersonal skills * Ability to work both independently and as part of a team * A positive attitude and strong work ethic * A full Irish Driving License (essential) **What We Offer** * Excellent training and career development opportunities * Hands\-on experience with modern telecom technologies * Supportive team environment * Company vehicle (where applicable) * Performance bonus * Bike to work scheme * Company pension * Employee discounts * Company events * On\-site parking If you’re looking to start your career in telecoms and want to work with a company that invests in your growth, apply today. Yellowcom is committed to promoting equal opportunities in employment. All applicants will be treated fairly based on merit, skills, and qualifications. Pay: €30,000\.00\-€35,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company car * Company events * Company pension * Employee assistance program * Employee discount * Free or subsidised travel * On\-site parking Application question(s): * Do you hold a full Irish driving licence? * Do you have any experience with telecoms, cabling, or IT/networking? * What interests you about a career in telecoms engineering? Work Location: On the road

title: KYB Analyst
description: **KYB Analyst (Consulting Role)** **Dublin** **About the Role** Albany Beck is seeking an experienced **KYB Analyst** to support a leading payments\-focused client operating in a fast\-paced, regulated environment. This role is ideal for someone with a strong background in business due diligence who enjoys working at the intersection of compliance, risk, and financial services innovation. You’ll be embedded within the client’s compliance team, playing a key role in onboarding, reviewing, and monitoring business customers while ensuring adherence to regulatory and internal standards. **Key Responsibilities** * Conduct end\-to\-end **KYB due diligence** on corporate and merchant customers * Assess complex ownership structures, UBOs, and control frameworks * Perform risk assessments across jurisdictions, industries, and customer types * Review and validate corporate documentation (e.g. incorporation docs, registries, financials) * Identify and escalate red flags, adverse media, and sanctions/PEP risks * Support ongoing monitoring, periodic reviews, and remediation activities * Collaborate closely with internal compliance, risk, and operations teams * Maintain clear, well\-documented case notes and audit\-ready records **Required Experience \& Skills** * Proven experience in **KYB / CDD / EDD** within payments, fintech, banking, or financial services * Strong understanding of AML/CTF regulations and financial crime risk * Ability to analyse complex corporate structures and cross\-border entities * Experience using KYB tools, registries, and screening platforms * High attention to detail with excellent written and verbal communication skills * Comfortable working in a consulting or client\-embedded environment **Desirable** * Experience supporting high\-growth fintech or payments firms * Familiarity with UK and international regulatory frameworks * Prior consulting or contractor experience

title: Kubernetes Engineer
description: **Overview** We are seeking a Kubernetes Engineer to join our global infrastructure team and help scale, automate, and secure our container orchestration environments across on\-premises and public cloud platforms. As a Kubernetes specialist, you’ll work closely with DevOps, SRE, and security teams to deliver reliable, self\-service, and production\-ready Kubernetes clusters that power mission\-critical applications. **Key Responsibilities** **Cluster Management** * Deploy, manage, and upgrade Kubernetes clusters using tools like kubeadm, EKS, AKS, GKE, or Rancher. * Implement robust RBAC, network policies, ingress controllers, and security frameworks in Kubernetes. **Automation \& IaC** * Automate cluster provisioning and application deployment pipelines using Terraform, Helm, and ArgoCD. * Build reusable modules for consistent infrastructure delivery across staging and production. **CI/CD Integration** * Integrate Kubernetes with modern CI/CD workflows for rapid, safe application delivery. * Support GitOps practices and continuous deployment automation. **Monitoring, Logging \& Troubleshooting** * Implement observability for Kubernetes using Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, and Fluentd/Fluent Bit. * Troubleshoot performance issues, failed pods, memory leaks, and cluster degradation events. **Cloud \& Hybrid Deployments** * Operate Kubernetes workloads across AWS, Azure, GCP, and hybrid/on\-prem environments. * Use tools like Velero, Kasten, or Stash for backup/restore strategies in Kubernetes. **Collaboration \& Support** * Partner with application developers, SREs, and security teams to implement best practices. * Serve as a technical advisor on cloud\-native architectures and containerization s **Experience/Qualification Required** * Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA) – Highly preferred * Certified Kubernetes Application Developer (CKAD) * Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist (CKS) * AWS Certified DevOps Engineer – Professional * Google Professional Cloud DevOps Engineer * HashiCorp Certified: Terraform Associate * Linux Foundation Certified System Administrator (LFCS) **Nice to have:** * Experience with GitOps tools (ArgoCD, Flux). * Observability tools like Prometheus, Grafana, ELK/EFK stack. * Experience with service mesh technologies (Istio, Linkerd). * Open\-source contributions in Kubernetes\-related projects * Spectro cloud, Incus, Cloud 9 etc… **About InterSystems** InterSystems, a creative data technology provider, delivers a unified foundation for next\-generation applications for healthcare, finance, manufacturing, and supply chain customers in more than 80 countries. Our data platforms solve interoperability, speed, and scalability problems for large organizations around the globe to unlock the power of data and allow people to perceive data in imaginative ways. Established in 1978, InterSystems is committed to excellence through its 24×7 support for customers and partners around the world. Privately held and headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts, InterSystems has 38 offices in 28 countries worldwide. For more information, please visit **InterSystems.com** .

description: **Who are we?** PSC Biotech is a leading Biotech Consultancy firm founded in 1996, headquartered in Pomona, California, USA, with Global operations in Ireland, India, Singapore, Australia and the US, serving 350 clients in more than 23 countries worldwide. We provide cloud\-based software solutions for Quality Management and Regulatory Inspections, pharmaceuticals contract manufacturing professionals, and metrology services to our clients. **‘Take your Career to a new Level’** PSC Biotech disrupts the conventional consultancy model by aligning our EVP as one of the unique selling point which includes the opportunity to work with the most talented cohort of like\-minded professionals operating in the Pharma/ Biotech Industry. We offer a permanent contract of employment giving exposure of working in Top Pharmaceutical client sites in a diverse\-cultural work setting. **Employee Value Proposition** Employees are the “heartbeat” of PSC Biotech, we provide unparalleled empowering career development though Learning \& Development in\-house training mentorship through constant guidance to facilitate career progression. We believe in creating high performing teams that can exceed our client’s expectations with regards to quality of all scalable and business unit deliverables, staying under budget and ensuring timelines for our deliverables are being met. Lab Analyst have the following responsibilities: * Accurately completing assigned analytical tests, examinations and procedures * Diligently completing all necessary quality records * Maintaining a clean and safe work environment by performing tasks in an organised and consistent fashion * Performing such other technical duties * Participate in such further training * Adhere to Health and Safety policy * Compliant to the ISO 17025, in relation to the testing, sampling, and calibration to collect reliable results. * Handling and preparation of chemical\-based reagents/buffers. * Assist in reviewing and developing Standard Operating Procedures * Ability to direct own work based upon lab priorities and deadlines * Lysing and cleaning samples * Genotyping * Samples quality checks * Making management aware of any issue **Requirements** Necessary qualifications/ training/ experience Degree/Diploma in Science or Equivalent Successful completion of internal induction training, as specified by the Lab Manger and described in the relevant training manual * Ability to thrive in a fast\-paced environment * Strong multitasking skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities effectively * Experience with DNA extraction methods is an advantage * Experience operating liquid handlers is highly desirable

title: Laboratory / Field Technician & Construction / Civil Engineers

Application can send their CV’s to hasan@mtsltd.ie Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €32,000\.00\-€36,500\.00 per year Benefits: * On\-site parking * Sick pay Ability to commute/relocate: * Carlow, County Carlow: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Language: * English (required) Licence/Certification: * EU Driving License (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Mainframe Application Developer
description: At U.S. Bank, we’re on a journey to do our best. Helping the customers and businesses we serve to make better and smarter financial decisions, enabling the communities we support to grow and succeed in the right ways, all more confidently and more often—that’s what we call the courage to thrive. We believe it takes all of us to bring our shared ambition to life, and each person is unique in their potential. A career with U.S. Bank gives you a wide, ever\-growing range of opportunities to discover what makes you thrive. Try new things, learn new skills and discover what you excel at—all from Day One. As a wholly owned subsidiary of U.S. Bank, Elavon is committed to building the platforms and ecosystems that help over 1\.5 million customers around the world to achieve their financial goals—no matter what they need. From transaction processing to customer service, to driving innovation and launching new products, we’re building a range of tailored payment solutions powered by the latest technology. As part of our team, you can explore what motivates and energizes your career goals: partnering with our customers, our communities, and each other. We actively uphold transparent and fair hiring practices that support individual opportunity, inclusive culture, and career mobility across all levels of our organisation. We offer meaningful opportunities for growth, a culture of inclusion, and a strong commitment to transparency and integrity in everything we do. **Job Description** ------------------- U.S. Bank is seeking a **Mainframe COBOL / CICS****DB2****Application Developer** with proven full SDLC experience to contribute toward the success of our technology initiatives. In this role you will be part of an Agile, DevOps team established to build, enhance and support our Merchant Acquiring System. You will consult with product owners, development leads and business owners on requirement gathering / refinement. You will be responsible for development, deployment and production support of a large, complex application. **As an Application Developer you will:** * Participate in all aspects of SDLC; design, coding, testing, implementation, and documentation of solutions of moderate complexity, significance, and risk. * Follow best practices of software development including, following coding standards, detailed and thorough testing, detailed documentation, and code\-reviews. * Create peer reviewed comprehensive technical designs, unit test plans and test cases. * Collaborate in team agile ceremonies, Daily Stand\-ups, refinement sessions, sprint planning. Provide clear and valuable input to discussions. Support colleagues, developers, test engineers and product owners. * Review, investigate and answer internal customer’s queries. * Troubleshoot and resolve production issues. * Join a 24x7 On\-Call rota, periodically providing out of hours support for our mainframe\-based applications. **Basic Qualifications****:** * Bachelor's degree, or equivalent work experience. * At least five years of experience with developing and implementing applications. **The following skills****and proven****experience with are required:** * mainframe COBOL / CICS * JCL, Batch Procedures and Parms * DB2 / SQL * IBM IDz * TSO * Batch scheduler e.g. Ca7 **The following s****kills****and proven****e****xperience****with****are advantageous:** * working within an agile methodology * IBM File Manager * VSAM * GIT * ServiceNow or other ITSM * Jira or other agile tool * Visual Studio * CoPilot or other AI development tools Prior knowledge of the payment industry or merchant acquiring is not required but is a distinct advantage. **Location expectations** This role requires working from a U.S. Bank location three (3\) or more days per week. **Accessibility** We are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment experience. If you need adjustments at any stage of the application or hiring process, please contact your recruiter for guidance and support. **Total Rewards** U.S. Bank is committed to fair, equitable, and transparent compensation practices in line with local regulatory and legal requirements. Our total rewards approach is designed to attract, retain, and support top talent while ensuring equal pay for work of equal value. We offer a market\-competitive compensation package that includes: * **Clearly defined salary ranges** aligned with industry benchmarks and internal equity standards. * **Performance\-based incentives** for eligible employees (as defined by relevant plan rules), awarded through transparent, objective criteria that recognize both individual and company performance. * **Inclusive equitable benefits** that are accessible to all employees and focused around our 3 main pillars of financial wellbeing, health \& wellness). * **Continuous development opportunities** including training, education support, and career progression pathways based on inclusive and transparent criteria. * **Employee recognition programs** that celebrate achievements and milestones for all. We regularly review our compensation and benefits to ensure they remain competitive, inclusive, and responsive to employee needs and market trends. Further details of the compensation package will be provided upon application. We encourage candidates to explore the full value of our offer, including monetary and non\-monetary benefits, at Employee benefits and development \| U.S. Bank \| Elavon. **Closing Date** Posting may be closed earlier due to high volume of applicants. We aim to provide timely updates throughout the process and encourage early applications to ensure consideration.

description: No Relocation Assistance Offered Job Number \#172769 \- Dublin, Dublin, Ireland **Who We Are** Colgate\-Palmolive Company is a global consumer products company operating in over 200 countries specializing in Oral Care, Personal Care, Home Care, Skin Care, and Pet Nutrition. Our products are trusted in more households than any other brand in the world, making us a household name! Join Colgate\-Palmolive, a caring, innovative growth company reimagining a healthier future for people, their pets, and our planet. Guided by our core values—Caring, Inclusive, and Courageous—we foster a culture that inspires our people to achieve common goals. Together, let's build a brighter, healthier future for all. **Marketing Technology Analyst, Digital Marketing GIT EMEA** **Help us transform the digital experience for pets and the people who care for them.** We are looking for a high\-energy **Marketing Technology** **Analyst** to join our GIT EMEA team. You will **lead** technology enablement for Data Acquisition, Marketing Automation and Omni\-Channel Personalisation for Hill’s EMEA across the pet and vet ecosystem. We aim to create a **seamless, personalized digital experience** that supports veterinarians in their practice and empowers pet parents in their journey. By harmonizing data across every touchpoint, we ensure that every interaction—whether in a clinic or on a smartphone—is relevant, timely, and impactful. **The Mission** 1P CRM data acquisition and activation is a key strategic driver for Hill’s EMEA. You will be the bridge between cutting\-edge technology and real\-world business value, working at the center of the Hill’s EMEA team, as part of the Global IT team, to craft a world\-class digital journey for vets and pet parents. **What You’ll Do** * Master the Data Lifecycle: Maintain a deep, end\-to\-end understanding of data flows across the entire ecosystem. You will ensure that data moves seamlessly between platforms with zero loss, maintaining high integrity from ingestion to activation. * Partner with Global Digital Applications teams to implement MarTech solutions across the entire professional and consumer journey. * Partner with the Hill’s EMEA team to drive acquisition, activation, and harmonization of first\-party data to fuel hyper\-personalized engagement. * Empower Local Markets: You’ll guide local markets on leveraging global platforms while ensuring their specific requirements are translated into technical reality. * Partner with CRM Managers: Work hand\-in\-hand with CRM leads to translate strategic objectives into technical requirements, ensuring data, journeys, and campaigns are built for success. * Be the Tech Ambassador: Translate "tech\-speak" into business value, driving tool adoption and ensuring stakeholders are aligned and inspired. * Safeguard the Experience: Lead regression testing across the ecosystem to ensure technical updates never compromise the user experience. * Document \& Train: Create the "source of truth" documentation and training materials to clarify roles and keep technical knowledge flowing across teams. * Manage the Flow: Navigate agile projects with ease, managing multiple assignments without losing sight of the details. **Who You Are** * Marketing\-Centric: You have a proven track record in Marketing, CRM and Media projects. You understand the "why" behind a campaign and how to speak the language of brand managers. * Technical Specialist: You are hands\-on with data modeling, complex analysis, and activation workflows. You don't just see data; you see the architecture required to move it. * A Solutions Hunter: You don’t just find bugs; you find better ways to work. You are self\-motivated, comfortable with ambiguity, and love to troubleshoot and experiment. * A Master Communicator: You can explain a roadmap to a developer and a business case to a Director with equal clarity. * Technically Sharp: You understand the "how" behind the "what," especially regarding data ingestion and harmonized ecosystems. **Your Toolkit** * Education: Degree in Business, IT, Computer Science, or Engineering. * Tech Literacy: Solid grasp of APIs, data modeling, data analysis, workflow and the digital marketing ecosystem. * Agile Mindset: Proven track record of managing digital initiatives and technology enablement in an agile environment. **Bonus Points For:** * A background in Email Marketing and activation strategy. * Hands\-on experience with Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Salesforce Data360, Gigya (Customer Data Cloud), and Snowflake. * Experience with CDP, Adobe Target, AEM, GA4 * Experience with Clean rooms; Infosum, Liveramp, Amazon **Knowledge of SQL, JavaScript, HTML, or CSS.** **Our Commitment to Inclusion** Our journey begins with our people—developing strong talent with diverse backgrounds and perspectives to best serve our consumers around the world and fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels a true sense of belonging. We are dedicated to ensuring that each individual can be their authentic self, is treated with respect, and is empowered by leadership to contribute meaningfully to our business. **Equal Opportunity Employer** Colgate is an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, ethnicity, age, disability, marital status, veteran status (United States positions), or any other characteristic protected by law. Reasonable accommodation during the application process is available for persons with disabilities. Please complete this request form should you require accommodation.
